posts.content:
2026-05-10

牧人心語

教會將於下星期日，五月十七日，下午二時三十分，在Milpitas 主堂舉行年中會員大會，並將會在會中選立匯點教會首批執事和修改部分會章。按立執事籌備委員和修章委員會將於星期三晚，五月十三日八時，舉行網上答問大會，歡迎關心教會的弟兄姊妹上網連線參加。會員在星期日必須親身出席會員大會，參與議事和投票。

五月二十九日，星期五晚八時，我們邀請到「泥土音樂」創辦人，知名基督徒詩歌創作人盛曉玫和她的團隊前來主領音樂佈道會。佈道會將於在Milpitas 主堂舉行，請把握機會邀請慕道友和喜歡基督徒詩歌的親友參加。

在Milpitas 主堂舉行的假期聖經學校已開始接受報名，是我們邀請未信主的鄰居、同事、親友把小孩子帶到教會認識福音的好機會。我們只餘下十幾個名額，請幫忙把電子海報轉發給親友們。

剛收到小苓傳道的短訊，告訴我星期六早上在Tracy的母親節崇拜有 60-70人出席，絕大部份都是慕道友。感謝讚美神！莊稼真的熟了，我們要求主呼喚更多肯承擔、願意出去收割的工人去收割。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 讚美並感謝神，祂經常用不同方式提醒我們要傳福音、收莊稼。求神讓我們成為被呼喚出去收割莊稼的工人。
  • 2. 為下主日的會員大會祈禱。求神讓關愛教會的弟兄姊妹在會議中同感一靈，順利選出教會第一批執事，以協助牧者們照顧、關心主內的弟兄姊妹。
  • 3. 為建議修改的會章能得到絕大部份會員認可，讓教會能更有效發展和推動教會事工祈禱。求聖靈賜智慧給會員，並感動大家按神心意通過修章內容。
  • 4. 為假期聖經學校招生祈禱。求神讓我們能利用這機會，引領更多慕道家庭到我們教會來認識基督的信仰和福音。
  • 5. 為五月廿九日晚在Milpitas主堂舉行的盛曉梅福音詩歌佈道會祈禱。求神讓我們能邀請更多慕道友參加佈道會，讓他們有機會聽信福音。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

The church will hold its mid-year membership meeting next Sunday, May 17th, at 2:30 PM in the Milpitas main campus. The meeting will include the election of the first batch of deacons and amendments to two articles of the church bylaws. The ordination planning committee and the bylaws revision committee will hold an online Q&A session on Wednesday evening, May 13th, at 8:00 PM. Brothers and sisters who care about the church are welcome to participate online. Members must attend the membership meeting in person on Sunday to participate in discussions and voting.

On Friday, May 29th, at 8:00 PM, we have invited Sheng Xiaomei, founder of “Clay Music” and a well-known Christian song writer and her team to lead a music evangelistic meeting. The evangelistic meeting will be held in the Milpitas main campus. Please take this opportunity to invite seekers and friends who enjoy Christian music to attend.

Registration for the Vacation Bible School held at the Milpitas main campus is now open for enrollment. This is a great opportunity to invite non-believing neighbors, colleagues, and relatives to bring their children to church to learn about the gospel. We only have a dozen or so spots left, please help forward the e-poster to your friends and family.

I just received a short message from Pastor Xiaoling, informing me that 60-70 people attended the Tracy’s Mother’s Day worship service on Saturday morning, the vast majority of whom are seekers. Praise be to God! The harvest is truly ripe; let us ask the Lord to call upon more willing and dedicated workers to reap the harvest.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise and thank God, who frequently reminds us to share the gospel and reap the harvest in different ways. Pray that God will call us to be the workers who go out to reap the harvest.
  • Pray for next Sunday’s membership meeting. Pray that God will make the brothers and sisters who care for the church be of one mind, so that we will successfully elect the first batch of church deacons to assist the pastors in caring for and supporting the brothers and sisters in Christ.
  • Pray that the proposed amendments to the church bylaws will be approved by the vast majority of church members, so that we can advance the ministry of the church in a more effective way. Pray that the Holy Spirit will grant wisdom to the members and move them to adopt the amendments according to God’s will.
  • Pray for the enrollment of the Vacation Bible School. Pray that God will enable us to use this opportunity to lead more families to our church to learn about Christ’s faith and the gospel.
  • Pray for the Gospel Song Evangelistic Meeting to be held on the evening of May 29th at the Milpitas main campus. Pray that God will enable us to invite more seekers to the meeting, so that they will have a chance to respond to God’s call for receiving the salvation.

posts.title: 2026-05-03-shepherdsheart
posts.excerpt: 2026-05-03 牧人心語 我要感謝讚美神，因為我再次經歷與弟兄姊妹同工的喜樂。星期四晚，我召開了今年第二次的全教會理事會議，主要議題是選舉出第一批教會執事候選人和相關的決議事項。雖然會議進行了兩個多小時，理事會的成員不單熱衷表達自己的意見和想法，更讓我感受到大家一起事奉神的積極與同心。感謝讚美神！ 教會議事會已選舉出八位執事候選人，敞若他們接受提名，將於下週向會眾公告，並在五月十七日的會員大會中由會員通過確認。除了選立執事和相關的議案，教會憲章修訂委員會亦將會提出會章需要修訂的事項。請教會會員預留時間出席參加。會員大會將於五月十七日下午二時卅分在Milpitas 主堂舉行，會員必須親身出席參與投票。 星期五晚，我經歷到天父的額外祝福。在我和師母離開San Leandro分堂時，在門口遇到一位特別來參加聚會的姊妹。她告訴師母剛從療養院探望父親回來，她父親的情況並不樂觀。於是師母請她帶我們去探望她父親。在路上才曉得她父親到九月就一百歲，仍未信主。我和師母到療養院後，發覺她父親竟然十分清醒，我就把握機會和他分享福音，鼓勵他認罪悔改，邀請耶穌做他的救主。他竟然一字一句，很清楚地跟我作了一個認罪悔改的祈禱，並不像一個垂死的人，他在我們的見證下接受了耶穌做救主。感謝讚美神！ 我們在離開San Leandro分堂時遇到這個姊妹是個偶遇，聖靈感動我們立刻跟她去療養院探望她父親是神的工作，要讓這位九十九歲的老人家有得救的機會；而這個姊妹的父親願意在極清醒的情況下認罪悔改，接受救恩，明顯是天父的祝福和聖靈的工作。我和師母卻有幸能與神同工，這真當天額外的祝福。聖經說：務要傳道，無論得時不得時。Amen! 招牧師 代禱事項 1. 讚美並感謝神，祂讓這位99歲的老人家有機會認識神的愛，最後接受了耶穌的救恩。讓我們祈求神的饒恕與平安從此與祂同在。 2. 為上週四教會理事會提名的執事候選人禱告，願他們蒙主悅納，甘願成為教會的領袖僕人。 3. 為神繼續供應教會的需要禱告，使我們有足夠的人力和物力在教會發展和建立主的事工上能力上加力。 4. 為我們的生命小組事工禱告，願它真正成為基督徒和教會成員實踐信仰、建立教會的平台。 5. 為正在經歷上人生挑戰和困難的弟兄姊妹禱告，願他們在困境中經歷神的同在和保護。祈求神讓他們能將一切憂慮交託給祂，並期待經歷神的恩典和祝福。 From the Heart of a Shepherd I want to […]
posts.content:
2026-05-03

牧人心語

我要感謝讚美神，因為我再次經歷與弟兄姊妹同工的喜樂。星期四晚，我召開了今年第二次的全教會理事會議，主要議題是選舉出第一批教會執事候選人和相關的決議事項。雖然會議進行了兩個多小時，理事會的成員不單熱衷表達自己的意見和想法，更讓我感受到大家一起事奉神的積極與同心。感謝讚美神！

教會議事會已選舉出八位執事候選人，敞若他們接受提名，將於下週向會眾公告，並在五月十七日的會員大會中由會員通過確認。除了選立執事和相關的議案，教會憲章修訂委員會亦將會提出會章需要修訂的事項。請教會會員預留時間出席參加。會員大會將於五月十七日下午二時卅分在Milpitas 主堂舉行，會員必須親身出席參與投票。

星期五晚，我經歷到天父的額外祝福。在我和師母離開San Leandro分堂時，在門口遇到一位特別來參加聚會的姊妹。她告訴師母剛從療養院探望父親回來，她父親的情況並不樂觀。於是師母請她帶我們去探望她父親。在路上才曉得她父親到九月就一百歲，仍未信主。我和師母到療養院後，發覺她父親竟然十分清醒，我就把握機會和他分享福音，鼓勵他認罪悔改，邀請耶穌做他的救主。他竟然一字一句，很清楚地跟我作了一個認罪悔改的祈禱，並不像一個垂死的人，他在我們的見證下接受了耶穌做救主。感謝讚美神！

我們在離開San Leandro分堂時遇到這個姊妹是個偶遇，聖靈感動我們立刻跟她去療養院探望她父親是神的工作，要讓這位九十九歲的老人家有得救的機會；而這個姊妹的父親願意在極清醒的情況下認罪悔改，接受救恩，明顯是天父的祝福和聖靈的工作。我和師母卻有幸能與神同工，這真當天額外的祝福。聖經說：務要傳道，無論得時不得時。Amen!

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 讚美並感謝神，祂讓這位99歲的老人家有機會認識神的愛，最後接受了耶穌的救恩。讓我們祈求神的饒恕與平安從此與祂同在。
  • 2. 為上週四教會理事會提名的執事候選人禱告，願他們蒙主悅納，甘願成為教會的領袖僕人。
  • 3. 為神繼續供應教會的需要禱告，使我們有足夠的人力和物力在教會發展和建立主的事工上能力上加力。
  • 4. 為我們的生命小組事工禱告，願它真正成為基督徒和教會成員實踐信仰、建立教會的平台。
  • 5. 為正在經歷上人生挑戰和困難的弟兄姊妹禱告，願他們在困境中經歷神的同在和保護。祈求神讓他們能將一切憂慮交託給祂，並期待經歷神的恩典和祝福。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

I want to thank and praise God for giving me the joy of working with brothers and sisters in Christ. On Thursday evening, I conducted the second church council meeting of the year, and the agendas were to nominate the first batch of church deacon candidates and related resolutions. Although the meeting lasted for more than two hours, the council members not only enthusiastically expressed their opinions and ideas, but also demonstrated an united spirit for the service to God.

The church council nominated eight deacon candidates. If they accept the nomination, they will be announced to the congregation next week and confirmed by members of the Church at the membership meeting on May 17th. In addition to the election of deacons and related resolutions, the church bylaws revision committee will also propose some amendments to the bylaws. Church members are requested to make time to attend the membership meeting which will be held at 2:30 pm on May 17th in the Milpitas main campus. Church members must attend in person to vote.

On Friday evening, I experienced an extra blessing from the Heavenly Father. As my wife and I were leaving the San Leandro campus, we met a sister in the parking lot who came for the evening fathering. She told my wife that she had just returned from visiting her father at the nursing home, and that his condition was not optimistic. So my wife asked her to take us to visit her father right awy. On the way, we learned that her father would be 100 years old in September and still hadn’t believed in the Lord. When my wife and I arrived at the nursing home, we found that her father was remarkably lucid. I seized the opportunity to share the Gospel with him, encouraging him to confess his sins, repent, and invite Jesus to be his Savior. It amazed us that he could clearly and distinctly confessed his sin in front of us, not like a dying man. He accepted Jesus as his Savior and Lord. Praise be to God!

Our meeting with this sister in Christ while we were leaving the San Leandro campus was an unplanned encounter. It was God’s grace that the Holy Spirit moved us to visit her father right away, giving this 99-year-old man a chance to receive salvation. And the fact that this sister’s father was willing to confess his sins, repent, and accept salvation while in a state of perfect lucidity was clearly a blessing from God and the work of the Holy Spirit. My wife and I were fortunate enough to work with God; this was truly an extra blessing to us that day. The Bible says: Preach the word, in season and out of season. Amen!

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise and thank God for giving the 99-year-old man an opportunity to learn about God’s love and end up to receiving the salvation from Jesus. Let us pray that God’s forgiveness and peace be with him from now on.
  • Pray for the deacon candidates nominated by the church council last Thursday that they would receive affirmation from the Lord and be willing to serve as lead servants of the Church.
  • Pray for God’s continued provision to the church so that we will have enough manpower and resources to develop and establish the ministry of the Lord at our church.
  • Pray for our life group ministry that it can indeed become a platform for Christians and church members to practice our faith and build up the Church.
  • Pray for God’s brothers and sisters in Christ who are undergoing life challenges and difficulties that they would experience God’s presence and His protection in their situation. Pray that they will be able to leave all their worries to God and look forward to experiencing God’s grace and blessings.

posts.title: 2026-04-26-shepherdsheart
posts.excerpt: 2026-04-26 牧人心語 轉眼間，San Leandro分堂已成立了四週年，他們將於這星期日一起切蛋糕慶祝。四月十九日，林修榮弟兄在那裡講道時，特別提到天父給匯點教會的恩典。他認為天父藉著San Leandro分堂的興起，激勵了不少教會的弟兄姊妹和牧者，並保守教會渡過危機。 當然，匯點教會今天仍屹立不倒，除了是天父恩典，興起了San Leandro分堂，但若缺少母堂和Pleasanton分堂弟兄姊妹的努力、體諒和積極支援，我們也不能走到今天。天父爸爸其實亦使用了匯點其他弟兄姊妹來重建教會，包括Tracy同半島的弟兄姊妹。 讓我藉San Leandro分堂慶祝成立四週年這個日子，鼓勵大家繼續同心協力建神家。天父爸爸知道我們的需要，已開始加添增強我們教會教牧團隊的實力。溫家樂傳道和梅漢偉傳道將於五月正式加入匯點服事，分別為Pleasanton分堂的全職牧師和Milpitas母堂的兼職牧師。現時我們仍在物色青少年英語牧師和青年中心傳道。求天父爸爸繼續激勵我們要萬眾一心，一起努力完成祂給我們教會的任務，成為人與神的交匯點。 星期五晚，李燕光牧師在國語聯合培靈會中，以「轉化荒年的奧秘」為題，激勵我們在面對個人靈性和教會事工的荒年中，將所有負面的情緒、感覺轉化為力量，找出突破看不足和缺欠的困，就必能經歷上帝的蔭庇和祝福。 我在Youtube上看到川普總統頌讀歷代志下七章十四節，說：「這稱為我名下的子民，若是自卑、禱告，尋求我的面，轉離他們的惡行，我必從天上垂聽，赦免他們的罪，醫治他們的地。」我對聖經的提醒說：阿們！也求聖靈讓我們都看到自己和國家的罪惡和得罪神的地方，並使我們願意謙卑悔改，使我們和我們的國家能得醫治。 招牧師 代禱事項 1. 感謝讚美神讓San Leandro Campus 渡過了令很多人振奮的四年。求神繼續使用這分堂的弟兄姊妹廣傳福音，引領更多決志信主。 2. 為Milpitas剛開始的國語門徒訓練代禱。求神使用黃傳道夫婦，栽培更多主內的弟兄姊妹成為愛主愛人的門徒。 3. 為Tracy的弟兄姊妹面正考慮變傳福音的策略代禱。求神加添智慧和所需要的人手，使他們能有效拓展那裡的福音事工。 4. 為即將於五月上任的溫家樂傳道和梅漢偉傳道代禱。求神預備他們的心，讓他們能早日適應匯點的文化，能有效地幫忙照顧和發展Pleasanton 和Milpitas這兩個堂會的事工。 5. 為中東危機能早日結束代禱。求神彰顯祂的公義，早日遏止邪惡極權國家遺害百姓的殘暴行動，並讓祂的大愛與憐憫早日降臨蒼涼之地。 From the Heart […]
posts.content:
2026-04-26

牧人心語

轉眼間，San Leandro分堂已成立了四週年，他們將於這星期日一起切蛋糕慶祝。四月十九日，林修榮弟兄在那裡講道時，特別提到天父給匯點教會的恩典。他認為天父藉著San Leandro分堂的興起，激勵了不少教會的弟兄姊妹和牧者，並保守教會渡過危機。

當然，匯點教會今天仍屹立不倒，除了是天父恩典，興起了San Leandro分堂，但若缺少母堂和Pleasanton分堂弟兄姊妹的努力、體諒和積極支援，我們也不能走到今天。天父爸爸其實亦使用了匯點其他弟兄姊妹來重建教會，包括Tracy同半島的弟兄姊妹。

讓我藉San Leandro分堂慶祝成立四週年這個日子，鼓勵大家繼續同心協力建神家。天父爸爸知道我們的需要，已開始加添增強我們教會教牧團隊的實力。溫家樂傳道和梅漢偉傳道將於五月正式加入匯點服事，分別為Pleasanton分堂的全職牧師和Milpitas母堂的兼職牧師。現時我們仍在物色青少年英語牧師和青年中心傳道。求天父爸爸繼續激勵我們要萬眾一心，一起努力完成祂給我們教會的任務，成為人與神的交匯點。

星期五晚，李燕光牧師在國語聯合培靈會中，以「轉化荒年的奧秘」為題，激勵我們在面對個人靈性和教會事工的荒年中，將所有負面的情緒、感覺轉化為力量，找出突破看不足和缺欠的困，就必能經歷上帝的蔭庇和祝福。

我在Youtube上看到川普總統頌讀歷代志下七章十四節，說：「這稱為我名下的子民，若是自卑、禱告，尋求我的面，轉離他們的惡行，我必從天上垂聽，赦免他們的罪，醫治他們的地。」我對聖經的提醒說：阿們！也求聖靈讓我們都看到自己和國家的罪惡和得罪神的地方，並使我們願意謙卑悔改，使我們和我們的國家能得醫治。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神讓San Leandro Campus 渡過了令很多人振奮的四年。求神繼續使用這分堂的弟兄姊妹廣傳福音，引領更多決志信主。
  • 2. 為Milpitas剛開始的國語門徒訓練代禱。求神使用黃傳道夫婦，栽培更多主內的弟兄姊妹成為愛主愛人的門徒。
  • 3. 為Tracy的弟兄姊妹面正考慮變傳福音的策略代禱。求神加添智慧和所需要的人手，使他們能有效拓展那裡的福音事工。
  • 4. 為即將於五月上任的溫家樂傳道和梅漢偉傳道代禱。求神預備他們的心，讓他們能早日適應匯點的文化，能有效地幫忙照顧和發展Pleasanton 和Milpitas這兩個堂會的事工。
  • 5. 為中東危機能早日結束代禱。求神彰顯祂的公義，早日遏止邪惡極權國家遺害百姓的殘暴行動，並讓祂的大愛與憐憫早日降臨蒼涼之地。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

In the blink of an eye, the San Leandro campus has been established for four years, and they will celebrate together this Sunday by cutting a cake. On April 19th, Brother Sau-Wing Lam, during his sermon there, specifically mentioned the grace of God the Father to Crosspoint Church. He believes that through the rise of the San Leandro campus, God the Father encouraged many brothers and sisters in Christ and the pastors, and protected the church through a crisis.

Of course, Crosspoint Church still stands strong today, not only because of God the Father’s grace in establishing the San Leandro campus, but also because of the efforts, understanding, and full support of the brothers and sisters in the main campus and the Pleasanton campus. God the Father also used other brothers and sisters at Crosspoint to rebuild the church, including the brothers and sisters in the Tracy and peninsula campuses.

Let me take this opportunity of celebrating the fourth anniversary of the San Leandro campus to encourage everyone to continue working together to build God’s house. God the Father knows our needs and has begun to strengthen our pastoral team by adding Pastors Caleb Wan to become our full-time pastor at the Pleasanton campus, and Joshua Moy as a part-time minister at the Milpitas main campus. We are currently still searching for a youth pastor and a youth center pastor. We pray that our Heavenly Father will continue to help unite us as one so that we can work together to fulfill the mission He has given to our church which is to become a cross point between man and God.

Last Friday evening, Pastor Lambert Li, in a joint Mandarin revival meeting, spoke on the topic of “The Mystery of Transforming a Famine Year,” encouraging us to transform all negative emotions and feelings into strength in the face of a famine in our personal spirituality and church ministry, finding ways to overcome our shortcomings and deficiencies, and thus experiencing God’s protection and blessings.

I saw President Trump reading 2 Chronicles 7:14 on YouTube, saying, “If my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray, seeking my face and turning from their wicked ways, I will hear from heaven and forgive their sin and heal their land.” I said Amen to this Bible reminder. I pray that the Holy Spirit will enable us to see the sins of ourselves and our nation that we have offended God. I pray that we will be willing to humble ourselves to repent, so that we and our nation may be healed.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • 1. Praise God for the inspiration that San Leandro Campus has given to so many brothers and sisters in Christ in the past 4 years. May God continue to use this mission base to spread the gospel and lead many more people to Christ.
  • 2. Pray for the newly started Mandarin disciple-making training in Milpitas. May God use Pastor Huang and his wife to cultivate more brothers and sisters in Christ into disciples who love the Lord and the people.
  • 3. Pray for the brothers and sisters in Tracy as they consider new strategies for evangelization. May God provide them with wisdom and the necessary manpower to expand the gospel ministry there.
  • 4. Pray for Pastor Caleb Wan and Joshua Moy, who will take office in May. May God prepare their hearts so they can quickly adapt to the culture of Crosspoint and help care for and develop the ministries in the Pleasanton and Milpitas campuses.
  • 5. Pray for an early end to the Middle East crisis. Pray that God will manifest His righteousness, stop the brutal actions of the evil totalitarian state that harm its people as soon as possible, and let His great love and mercy descend upon the desolate land.

posts.content:
牧人心語

有一對已移居洛杉機多年的前匯點人夫婦，星期二晚約我在線上會面。他們和我分享搬到羅省後，就一直參加這個教會的聚會，但愈來愈感到很難投入在這個教會中。特別最近他們主動和牧師聯絡，開始參與教會服事後，他們就愈發感到是外人，沒有被看為是「家」裡的人。

我問他們為甚麼會有這樣的感覺？他們說，很多弟兄姊妹和他們一樣，只是回來教會幫忙服事，彼此間沒有團契溝通；他們亦不清楚教會想要怎麼發展，大家都只是奉命而行。如果他們不是主動聯絡牧師，他們仍然只是來參加敬拜聚會的人。

每個教會都有一些只是來參加敬拜聚會的人，並沒有把教會看作是自己屬靈的家。所以，我為這對年輕夫婦能主動找牧師尋找服事機會感謝神。保羅提醒我們：你們不再作外人和客旅，是與聖徒同國，是神家裡的人了。（以弗所書 2:9）

耶穌升天後在地上設立教會，讓每個相信祂，願意跟隨祂，服事祂的人都有一個屬靈的「家」，讓他能在「家」裡和其他弟兄姊妹一起成長，被建立成為像主的門徒，和可以完成基督使命的軍旅，讓我們可以和祂一起搶救失落的靈魂。但我發覺有些弟兄姊妹並不明白保羅這個提醒的意義，只把教會看作為一個讓基督徒來聚會的地方，並且這裡有甚麼不好、有那些不是。

事實上，每個教會都有自己需要面對的問題。但當我們發覺教會有問題時，我們是袖手旁觀？大喊大罵？離家出走？還是一起想方法解決問題，建造一個能榮耀神的「家」呢？

我希望大家把匯點看作你屬靈的家，讓自己在這裡可以和其他弟兄姊妹互助成長，一起面對困難和挑戰，完成神交付給教會的使命。主內的弟兄姊妹、匯點人，讓我們再次成為人與神之間的交匯點！

這個星期五（四月廿四日）晚八點鐘，我們在San Leandro分堂有國語聯合培靈會。講員為華人策略聯盟總幹事李燕光牧師。講題為 「轉化荒年的奧秘。」有即時中文字幕翻譯。歡迎弟兄姊妹出席參加。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神San Leandro Campus 有一百位小朋友出席復活節的尋蛋遊戲，並已有卅多人報名參加這星期日舉辦的手工班。求神使用這機會引領更多慕道家庭到教會認識神。
  • 2. 為星期五晚的國語培靈會代禱。求神使用講員的信息激勵會眾追求靈命成長，把握機會服事神。
  • 3. 為我們當中的基督徒家長代禱。求神加添智慧和敏銳的心，懂得如何與子女溝通和幫助他們進一步認識神。
  • 4. 為仍在尋找工作的弟兄姊妹代禱。求神賜平安，並指引他們尋找工作的方向和方法。
  • 5. 為青少年暑期短宣的籌備工作代禱。求神幫助Pastor Hankin和他的團隊在籌募經費和安排短宣各項事宜上都順利妥當，讓參加者得造就。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

A couple, former members of the Crosspoint Church who had lived in Los Angeles for 6-7 years, met with me online Tuesday evening. They shared with me that since they moved to Los Angeles, they have been attending a church. However, they increasingly felt a lack of engagement after they proactively contacted the pastor and began to serve as a greeter on Sunday. They felt like outsiders, not considered as part of the church family.

I asked them why they felt this way. They said that many brothers and sisters were like them, only coming to church to help with some works, but without much fellowship or communication. They also didn’t understand how the church wanted to develop. Everyone was following orders from the pastor. In fact, if they hadn’t proactively contacted the pastor, they would still just be regular attendees to the worship service.

Every church has some people who only attend worship services and don’t see the church as their spiritual home. I thanked God for this young couple because they took the initiative in seeking out opportunities to serve. Paul reminds us: You are no longer strangers or foreigners, but fellow citizens with God’s people, members of God’s household. (Ephesians 2:9)

After Jesus ascended to heaven, He established the church on earth, providing a spiritual “home” for everyone who believes in Him. This “home” allows them to grow together with other brothers and sisters in Christ, to be built up to become Christlike disciples, and part of the army capable of fulfilling Christ’s mission in rescuing the lost souls. However, I noticed that some brothers and sisters don’t understand the meaning of Paul’s reminder. They see the church merely as a place for Christians to gather, and they disapprove this and that of the church.

Indeed, every church has its own problems. When we discover problems in the church, do we stand idly by? Shout and yell? Run away? Or do we work together to find solutions and build a “home” that glorifies God? Yes, it depends on how we consider the church as our spiritual “home.”

I pray that you will see Crosspoint Church as your spiritual home, a place where you can grow in Christ, help you face difficulties and challenges, and be enabled to fulfill the mission God entrusted to the church. Brothers and sisters, members of Crosspoint Church, let us once again become a cross point between man and God!

This Friday (April 24th) at 8 PM, we will be holding a joint Mandarin revival meeting at the San Leandro Campus. The speaker will be Pastor Lambert Li, General Secretary of the Chinese Strategic Alliance. The topic of his talk is “The Mystery of Transforming Famine Years.” Instant Chinese subtitles will be provided. All brothers and sisters are welcome to attend.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise be to God that one hundred children attended the Easter egg hunt at the San Leandro Campus. Over thirty of them have registered for this Sunday’s craft class. Pray that God will use this opportunity to lead more families seeking to know Christ at our church.
  • Pray for the Friday evening’s Mandarin revival meeting. Pray that God will use the speaker’s message to inspire the congregation to pursue spiritual growth and seize opportunities to serve God.
  • Pray for the Christian parents among us. Pray that God will add wisdom and sensitivity to their hearts while they try to communicate with their children and help them further know God.
  • Pray for the brothers and sisters still looking for job. Pray that God will grant them peace and guide them in their job search.
  • Pray for the preparations for the summer mission trip for our teenagers. Pray that God will help Pastor Hankin and his team to successfully raise funds and arrange all aspects of the mission trip, so that the youth will be edified.

posts.content:
牧人心語

從這星期開始，我們將開始一個新的講道系列：讓我們的家成為一個門徒訓練中心。

我們教會，不是有個匯點培訓中心嗎？為甚麼教會把這個培訓門徒的責任丟到家裡去？但事實上，是我們把在家裡培育信徒的責任丟給了教會。

摩西領受十誡之後，就招聚百姓，向他們頒布和講解十誡。然後對他們說：「我今日所吩咐你的話都要記在心上，也要殷勤教訓你的兒女。無論你坐在家裡，行在路上，躺下，起來，都要談論。」（申命記 6: 6-7）

今日有很多年青人離開教會和信仰，就是因為教會未能有效幫助教會裡的家庭成為門徒訓練中心，讓我們栽培屬靈生命成長的基地失守了。教會未能裝備家長做屬靈導師，甚至讓家長們以為這是教會的責任。有些家長不是不願意培訓自己的孩子，但是有心無力，也不知如何開始。有些家長認為每個人都有自己的路要走，更忽略自己的言語、生活、行動，對子女屬靈生命造成的影響。

我很希望藉著這個講道系列，將神對家庭和家長的期望陳明，幫助教會裡的弟兄姊妹能重建家庭祭壇，神在家中居首位，使我們的家能成為造就門徒、裝備福音使者的訓練中心。

請鼓勵主內的弟兄姊妹一同出席敬拜，聆聽神的教導；並轉發講道的錄影頻道給缺席的親友們觀看學習。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神賜給我們一個蒙福的復活節主日。祈求神讓基督復活的大能蔭庇我們，讓我們能面對挑戰、勝過試探，天天見證主恩。
  • 2. 為中東停火協議和談判能帶來中東和平和穏定世界經濟代禱。求神幫助世人明白是祂在掌管一切，讓他們樂意認識神。
  • 3. 為正在接受培訓的教會執事候選人代禱。求神鞏固建立他們的靈性，並有謙卑追求進一步認識神、服事神的心志。
  • 4. 為招牧師正展開教牧同工的培訓祈禱。求神加添精神體力給招牧師和眾教牧同工，讓他們在知識、悟性和屬靈的見識、體會上不斷增長，能更有效牧養會眾。
  • 5. 為假期聖經學校和運動日營的籌備工作代禱。求神興起領袖和招聚足夠義工幫忙策劃推動這兩個特別事工。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

Starting this Sunday, we will begin a new sermon series: Let our home become disciple-making center.

Our church has a Crosspoint Training Center, right? Why is the church shifting the responsibility of disciple-making to our homes? But in fact, it is we who are shifting the responsibility of nurturing believers at home to the church.

After Moses received the Ten Commandments, he gathered the people together and gave them the commandments and explained to them. Then he said to them, “These commands I give you today shall be on your hearts; be diligent in teaching them to your children. Talk about them whether you are sitting at home or walking by the road, lying down or getting up.” (Deuteronomy 6: 6-7)

Many young people today leave the church and their faith because the church has failed to help families within the church become disciple-making centers, causing us to lose the base for nurturing spiritual growth. The church has failed to equip parents as spiritual mentors, even leading parents to believe that this is the church’s responsibility. Some parents are not unwilling to train up their children, but they are unequipped to do so, that they do not know where to begin. Some parents believe everyone has their own path to walk, neglecting the impact of their words, lifestyle, and actions have on their children’s spiritual lives.

I sincerely hope that through this sermon series, God’s expectations for families and parents can be clarified, helping brothers and sisters in the church to rebuild their family altars, ensuring God’s place of first importance in the home, so that we can make our homes training centers for discipleship and equipping messengers of the gospel.

Please encourage brothers and sisters in Christ to attend our worship services and listen to God’s teachings. Please also share the recorded sermons with relatives and friends who are unable to attend.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • We thank and praise God for giving us a blessed Easter Sunday. Let’s pray that God will allow the power of Christ’s resurrection to protect us, enabling us to face challenges, overcome temptations, and witness to the Lord’s grace every day.
  • Let us pray that the Middle East ceasefire agreement and peace negotiations will bring peace to the Middle East and stabilize the world economy. Let us ask God to help people understand that He is in control of everything, so that they will be willing to know Him more.
  • Let us pray for the potential church deacon candidates who are currently undergoing training. Let us ask God to strengthen and build up their spirituality and to cultivate in them a humble spirit to further understand and serve God.
  • Let us pray for Pastor Chiu’s ongoing training of pastoral staff. Let us ask God to provide Pastor Chiu and all the pastoral staff with spiritual and physical strength, so that they may continuously grow in knowledge, understanding, and spiritual insight and experience, enabling them to shepherd their congregations in more effective ways.
  • Let us pray for the preparations for the Vacation Bible School and the Sports Camp. Let us ask God to raise up adequate leaders and gather enough volunteers to help plan and promote these two special ministries.

posts.title: 2026-04-05-shepherdsheart
posts.excerpt: 牧人心語 我參加了星期五晚在Milpitas主堂舉行的受苦節崇拜。這次聚會，由容牧師、Pastor Hankin和黃正傳道統籌和安排。他們每個細節都留意到，我很感恩有一群年青的牧者來負責這個聚會。 Milpitas受苦節的崇拜以邀請會眾自己安靜默禱結束。我留到最後，並觀察到最後離開的，竟是幾個青少年女孩。我們對年青人的印象，通常是坐不定、經常跳崩崩的。但我卻看到她們是很安靜的坐在坐位上。我希望她們真的在默念耶穌基督為我們受苦受死所顯示的愛和帶給我們的盼望。 我坐在我的位置上，心裡沒有禱告，也沒有思想甚麼經文或耶穌受苦的故事。我只是安靜的坐在那裡，看著熒光幕上埋葬耶穌的墳墓，讓自己去感受一下坐在耶穌墳墓門口那種滋味，盡在不言中。我是坐在 21世紀教堂裡面的坐位上，但我的心是在兩千多年前耶穌的墳墓外。我是在記念耶穌的受苦受死，還是期待已復活的救主向我顯現？ 這個星期日，我是大家來參加復活節崇拜的時候，不是單單來慶祝耶穌已經從死裡復活，乃是來朝見已經復活的主，祈求祂以復活的大能，幫助我們在這末世為祂而活。 招牧師 代禱事項 1. 感謝讚美神為我們預備有新牧者將會加入教會服事。祈求神繼續強化我們的教牧事奉團隊，讓教會能更有效完成使命。 2. 請為戰爭帶給世界的不安、甚至恐慌祈禱。求神讓更多人看到平安感不是來自外在的環境，乃是來自我們對人生的把握。若我們人生的把握是來自上帝，我們就有平安。 3. 為最近來參加我們各堂點活動的新朋友代禱。求神讓他們感受到神的愛和我們的關心，並從神話語中得安慰和醫治。 4. 為在復活節受浸的弟兄姊妹代禱。求神加添他們的信心，生命被聖靈繼續轉化，生命的見證能吸引更多人渴慕追求認識神。 5. 為教會宣教部正檢視本地和國外的宣教事工代禱。求神賜給宣教委員們智慧和洞察力，能明白神的心意和制定教會的宣教策略。 From the Heart of a Shepherd I attended the Good Friday […]
posts.content:
牧人心語

我參加了星期五晚在Milpitas主堂舉行的受苦節崇拜。這次聚會，由容牧師、Pastor Hankin和黃正傳道統籌和安排。他們每個細節都留意到，我很感恩有一群年青的牧者來負責這個聚會。

Milpitas受苦節的崇拜以邀請會眾自己安靜默禱結束。我留到最後，並觀察到最後離開的，竟是幾個青少年女孩。我們對年青人的印象，通常是坐不定、經常跳崩崩的。但我卻看到她們是很安靜的坐在坐位上。我希望她們真的在默念耶穌基督為我們受苦受死所顯示的愛和帶給我們的盼望。

我坐在我的位置上，心裡沒有禱告，也沒有思想甚麼經文或耶穌受苦的故事。我只是安靜的坐在那裡，看著熒光幕上埋葬耶穌的墳墓，讓自己去感受一下坐在耶穌墳墓門口那種滋味，盡在不言中。我是坐在 21世紀教堂裡面的坐位上，但我的心是在兩千多年前耶穌的墳墓外。我是在記念耶穌的受苦受死，還是期待已復活的救主向我顯現？

這個星期日，我是大家來參加復活節崇拜的時候，不是單單來慶祝耶穌已經從死裡復活，乃是來朝見已經復活的主，祈求祂以復活的大能，幫助我們在這末世為祂而活。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神為我們預備有新牧者將會加入教會服事。祈求神繼續強化我們的教牧事奉團隊，讓教會能更有效完成使命。
  • 2. 請為戰爭帶給世界的不安、甚至恐慌祈禱。求神讓更多人看到平安感不是來自外在的環境，乃是來自我們對人生的把握。若我們人生的把握是來自上帝，我們就有平安。
  • 3. 為最近來參加我們各堂點活動的新朋友代禱。求神讓他們感受到神的愛和我們的關心，並從神話語中得安慰和醫治。
  • 4. 為在復活節受浸的弟兄姊妹代禱。求神加添他們的信心，生命被聖靈繼續轉化，生命的見證能吸引更多人渴慕追求認識神。
  • 5. 為教會宣教部正檢視本地和國外的宣教事工代禱。求神賜給宣教委員們智慧和洞察力，能明白神的心意和制定教會的宣教策略。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

I attended the Good Friday worship service at Milpitas Campus on Friday evening. The service was organized and arranged by Pastor Yung, Pastor Hankin, and Pastor Zheng Huang. They paid attention to every detail, and I am very grateful to have such a group of young pastors in charge of this gathering.

The Milpitas Good Friday worship service ended with an invitation for the congregation to pray quietly. I stayed until the end and observed that the last to leave were several teenage girls. Our impression of young people is usually that they are restless and often jumping around. But I saw them sitting quietly in their seats. I hope they were truly reflecting on the love shown by Jesus Christ in his suffering and death for us and the hope he brings us.

I sat in my seat, not praying, not thinking about any scriptures or the story of Jesus’ suffering. I simply sat there quietly, watching the image of Jesus’ tomb on the screen, trying to feel what it was like to sit outside of Jesus’ tomb—a feeling beyond words. I sit in a seat inside a 21st-century church, but my heart is outside the tomb of Jesus, more than two thousand years ago. Am I remembering Jesus’ suffering and death, or am I waiting for the resurrected Savior to appear to me?

This Sunday, I pray that we come to Easter worship not merely to celebrate Jesus’ resurrection, but to meet the resurrected Lord. I pray that He, with the power of His resurrection, will help us live for Him in these trouble days.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise God for preparing new pastors to join our church ministry. Pray that God will continue to strengthen our pastoral team, enabling them to help the church fulfill its mission more effectively.
  • Pray for the unease and even panic that war brings to the world. Ask God to help more people see that peace does not come from external circumstances, but from our control over our lives. If our control over our lives comes from God, we will have peace.
  • Pray for the new friends who have recently attended our church activities. Ask God to let them feel God’s love and our care, and to find comfort and healing in God’s Word.
  • Pray for the brothers and sisters baptized on Easter Sunday. Ask God to strengthen their faith, that their lives may continue to be transformed by the Holy Spirit, and that their testimony may attract more people to thirst for and seek to know God.
  • Pray for the church’s mission department as it reviews its local and international mission work. Ask God to grant the mission committee members wisdom and insight to understand God’s will and formulate mission strategies for the church.

posts.content:
牧人心語

在過去兩週，我分別約見了兩三位傳道人，和他們分享教會的現況和需要，邀請他們考慮加入匯點教會服事。與此同時，我亦和約二十位符合資格被提名的執事候選人聯絡，確認他們是願意接受執事候選人的訓練。我要感謝讚美上帝，因為這些弟兄姊妹都很認真去為被邀請這件事祈禱，然後才做出決定。

這些被邀請的弟兄姊妹不單有事奉的心，更有敬畏上帝、愛主的心，沒有一個覺得自己完全合資格和能夠勝任，但願意聽從主的吩咐，謙卑地學習事奉，和其他弟兄姊妹一起建立教會。他們將於復活節當天開始接受培訓，為期六週。教會的理事會將會在其中邀請部分候選人被提名成為執事候選人，於五月中的會員大會中由會員投票確立。請大家為他們祈禱。

四月三日受苦節晚上，教會分別在Milpitas, Pleasanton, San Leandro三個堂點舉行特別崇拜，記念耶穌基督的犧牲和所彰顯的大愛，激勵我們在這末世，也願意為主受苦，完成天父交付給我們的任務。請弟兄姊妹預留時間出席參加。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神讓一班愛主的弟兄姊妹願意接受執事訓練，考慮被按立成為教會執事。請為他們的心志祈禱，祈求神繼續引領他們的決定。
  • 2. 請繼續為中東的戰爭能早日結束祈禱。我們都不願意看到生靈塗炭，但求上帝的旨意成就，為世界帶來更大好處。
  • 3. 為受苦節和復活節在各個堂會舉行的記念活動和外展活動代禱。求神讓我們在這個復活節的週末裡，能更深體驗神的愛，並能把這愛傳給別人。
  • 4. 為即將於復活節當天受浸的弟兄姊妹代禱。求神繼續模造和帶領他們人生的道路，經歷神的恩典和大愛更多。浸禮將在San Leandro 分堂舉行。英語浸禮則於四月廿六日在Milpitas主堂舉行。
  • 5. 為教會繼續物色傳道同工祈禱。祈求神將合適的牧者帶到我們當中，與其他牧者一同牧養和發展教會。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

Over the past two weeks, I met with a few pastors to share the church’s current situation and needs, inviting them to consider joining Crosspoint Church in ministry. At the same time, I have also contacted about twenty qualified deacon candidates to confirm their willingness to undergo deacon candidate training. I thank and praise God that these brothers and sisters in Christ prayed earnestly about being invited before making their decision.

These brothers and sisters not only have a heart for serving at the Church, but also a heart of reverence for God and love for the Lord. None of them feel fully qualified or capable, but they are willing to obey the Lord’s commands, humbly learn to serve, and build the church together with other brothers and sisters. They will begin their six-week training on Easter Sunday. The church council members will invite some of these candidates to be nominated as deacon candidates, and the church members will vote on them at the membership meeting in mid-May. Please pray for them.

On the evening of Good Friday, April 3rd, the church will hold special worship services at three locations: Milpitas, Pleasanton, and San Leandro. These services will help us commemorate the sacrifice of Jesus Christ and the great love he revealed. I pray that these activities will inspire us to also be willing to suffer for the Lord in these last days and fulfill the tasks entrusted to us by our Heavenly Father. Brothers and sisters are encouraged to make time to attend these activities.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise be to God that a group of brothers and sisters who love the Lord have been willing to undergo deacon training and consider being ordained as church deacons. Please pray for their hearts and ask God to continue to guide their decision.
  • Please continue to pray for an early end to the war in the Middle East. We all do not wish to see bloodshed, but let us pray for God’s will be done to bring greater good to the world.
  • Pray for the commemorative and outreach activities to be held in various church campuses for Good Friday and Easter. Ask God to help us experience God’s love more deeply this Easter weekend and to share that love with others.
  • Pray for the brothers and sisters who will be baptized on Easter Sunday. Ask God to continue to mold and guide them on their life’s path, so that they may experience more of God’s grace and love. The baptism will be held at the San Leandro Campus. The English baptism will be held on April 26th at the Milpitas main campus.
  • Pray for the continue search for pastoral staff. Let us pray that God will bring suitable ones to us so that they may help shepherd and develop the church together with other pastors.

posts.title: 2026-03-22-shepherdsheart
posts.excerpt: 牧人心語 前兩天，我約了一位弟兄見面，分享我們在信仰上的經歷和上帝在我們生命中的帶領。他六十多歲，已經退休了兩次，但他對我說，現在人的壽命，特別在醫藥發達的美國，估計很多人可以活到一百二十歲。六、七十歲的人只是進入人生的中年，很多在這個年齡層的人仍然充滿活力，可以繼續服事神，做很多事情。所以，他的態度是，只要神給他機會，他很樂意服事，讓他餘下的生命活得更有意義。 這位弟兄的分享給我很大鼓勵。能夠服事上帝，真的是很大的福氣，更是讓我們能更親近神、更多經歷神的時候。我們今天仍然活著，不知我們生活的重心是放在那裡？我們是為自己而活還是為他人而活？我們有想過要為神而活嗎？為神而活又是甚麼意思？ 歌羅西書三章 27和13節那裡說：無論做甚麼，都要從心裡做，像是給主做的，不是給人做的⋯⋯無論做甚麼，或說話或行事，都要奉主耶穌的名，藉著他感謝父神。 招牧師 代禱事項 1. 感謝讚美神讓我們可以不像世人一般活在世上。感謝讚美神賜給我們更崇高的人生目標和理想。求神藉著聖靈繼續引領我們在世上的生活。 2. 為中東戰火能早日平息祈禱，也為全世界受影響的萬千群眾祈禱。求神讓我們在苦難和危機中曉得倚靠祂，並能重新得力面對現實人生。 3. 為受苦節和復活節分別在各個堂會舉行的紀念活動和外展活動代禱。我們需要各堂會的弟兄姊妹參與和幫忙推廣這些聚會和活動，讓復活節的週末變得更有意義。 4. 為San Leandro分堂將於下主日(29日)開始英語崇拜祈禱。求神祝福這事工，讓我們能吸收更多當地說英文的群體參加教會的聚會。 5. 為Pleasanton分堂將於四月開始的星期六青少年事工祈禱。求神讓更多家長能參與這事工，藉著陳牧師和負責弟兄的領導拓展青少年事工。 From the Heart of a Shepherd A couple of days ago, I […]
posts.content:
牧人心語

前兩天，我約了一位弟兄見面，分享我們在信仰上的經歷和上帝在我們生命中的帶領。他六十多歲，已經退休了兩次，但他對我說，現在人的壽命，特別在醫藥發達的美國，估計很多人可以活到一百二十歲。六、七十歲的人只是進入人生的中年，很多在這個年齡層的人仍然充滿活力，可以繼續服事神，做很多事情。所以，他的態度是，只要神給他機會，他很樂意服事，讓他餘下的生命活得更有意義。

這位弟兄的分享給我很大鼓勵。能夠服事上帝，真的是很大的福氣，更是讓我們能更親近神、更多經歷神的時候。我們今天仍然活著，不知我們生活的重心是放在那裡？我們是為自己而活還是為他人而活？我們有想過要為神而活嗎？為神而活又是甚麼意思？

歌羅西書三章 27和13節那裡說：無論做甚麼，都要從心裡做，像是給主做的，不是給人做的⋯⋯無論做甚麼，或說話或行事，都要奉主耶穌的名，藉著他感謝父神。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神讓我們可以不像世人一般活在世上。感謝讚美神賜給我們更崇高的人生目標和理想。求神藉著聖靈繼續引領我們在世上的生活。
  • 2. 為中東戰火能早日平息祈禱，也為全世界受影響的萬千群眾祈禱。求神讓我們在苦難和危機中曉得倚靠祂，並能重新得力面對現實人生。
  • 3. 為受苦節和復活節分別在各個堂會舉行的紀念活動和外展活動代禱。我們需要各堂會的弟兄姊妹參與和幫忙推廣這些聚會和活動，讓復活節的週末變得更有意義。
  • 4. 為San Leandro分堂將於下主日(29日)開始英語崇拜祈禱。求神祝福這事工，讓我們能吸收更多當地說英文的群體參加教會的聚會。
  • 5. 為Pleasanton分堂將於四月開始的星期六青少年事工祈禱。求神讓更多家長能參與這事工，藉著陳牧師和負責弟兄的領導拓展青少年事工。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

A couple of days ago, I met with a brother to share our faith journey and God’s guidance in our lives. He is in his sixties and has retired twice, but he told me that with today’s advanced medical care, especially in the United States, many people can probably live to be 120. Those in their sixties and seventies are just entering middle age, and many in this age group are still full of energy. They can continue to serve God and do many things. Therefore, his attitude is that as long as God gives him the opportunity, he is happy to serve, making the rest of his life more meaningful.

This brother’s sharing greatly encouraged me. Being able to serve God is truly a great blessing, and it allows us to draw closer to God and pray to Him more often. We are still alive today, but where is our focus in life? Are we living for ourselves or for others? Have we thought about living for God? And what does it mean to live for God?

Colossians 3:27-13 says: “Whatever you do, do it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for human beings…Whatever you do, whether you speak or act, do it in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.”

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise God for allowing us to live a life unlike ordinary people. Praise God for giving us higher goals and ideals in life. May God continue to guide us in our lives through the Holy Spirit.
  • Pray for an early end to the war in the Middle East, and pray for the millions of people affected worldwide. May God help us to rely on Him in times of suffering and crisis, and to regain strength to face the reality of life.
  • Pray for the commemorative and outreach activities to be held in various church campuses at the Easter weekend. We need the participation and help of the brothers and sisters in various church campuses to help promote these events, making the Easter weekend more meaningful and memorable.
  • Pray for the English worship service to begin next Sunday (the 29th) at the San Leandro campus. May God bless this ministry, enabling us to attract more local English-speaking people to the church.
  • Pray for the Saturday youth ministry to begin at the Pleasanton campus in April. Let us pray that God will bring more parents to participate in this ministry, and that the youth ministry will be expanded through the leadership of Pastor Chan and the responsible brother.

posts.title: 2026-03-15-shepherdsheart
posts.excerpt: 牧人心語 我和師母這次回香港探親是來去匆匆，而且行程緊密，連一起長大的教會朋友，都未能安排到見面的機會。星期六晚回來，星期日早講道，隨之而來是一連串的教會活動、會議、和探訪，連和兒孫們見面吃飯都要改期，真是忙得不亦樂乎。 是的，我們真是忙得不亦樂乎。我可以想像如果大家家裡還有小朋友和老人家，可能就會更忙了。不過最近我和師母都有同感，我們大部份時間都是為天父上帝而忙碌，因為我們是全職傳道人，而天父爸爸竟然讓我們這把年紀仍有生命和精力去服事祂，其實是祂的厚愛和恩典。 有人感嘆人生苦短，意思是人生太短暫，轉瞬即逝，若不好好珍惜、享受當下，人生就會虛度，所以我們應該及時行樂，過自己喜歡的生活。但正如摩西所言：我們一生的年日是七十歲，若是強壯可到八十歲；但其中所矜誇的不過是勞苦愁煩，轉眼成空，我們便如飛而去。到底是甚麼令我們的人生過得更有意義？ 所羅門王總結他對人生的觀察，說：你們一切都聽見了，總而言之，應當敬畏神，謹守他的誡命，因為這是每一個人的本分。人所作的每一件事，連一切隱藏的事，無論是善是惡，神都必審問。（傳道書 12:13-14） 所以，愛耶和華你們的神，行他一切的道，守他的誡命，專靠他，盡心盡性事奉他。（約書亞記 22:5） 招牧師 代禱事項 1. 感謝讚美神讓我們仍有能力去服事祂。求神加添我們智慧和信心，讓我們懂得如何安排時間，把握機會去服事祂，更深去經歷神的愛和恩典。 2. 為美國和以色列的國家領袖祈禱。求神觸動他們的心，讓他們曉得他們對國人和全世界的責任，設法早日結束戰爭。 3. 為受苦節和復活節分別在各個堂會舉行的活動代禱。讓我們在復活節期間能有機會安靜反思基督復活帶給我們的盼望，讓我們能重新得力，面對人生的挑戰，完成我們在世上的使命。 4. 為San Leandro分堂將於三月底開始英語崇拜的籌備工作祈禱。求神祝福，把更多要聽英語的弟兄姊妹和慕道友帶到神面前。 5. 為Pleasanton的青少年事工祈禱。求神預備合適的同工來幫忙，並激勵家長能同心合意建立我們的青少年事工。 From the Heart of a Shepherd My wife and […]
posts.content:
牧人心語

我和師母這次回香港探親是來去匆匆，而且行程緊密，連一起長大的教會朋友，都未能安排到見面的機會。星期六晚回來，星期日早講道，隨之而來是一連串的教會活動、會議、和探訪，連和兒孫們見面吃飯都要改期，真是忙得不亦樂乎。

是的，我們真是忙得不亦樂乎。我可以想像如果大家家裡還有小朋友和老人家，可能就會更忙了。不過最近我和師母都有同感，我們大部份時間都是為天父上帝而忙碌，因為我們是全職傳道人，而天父爸爸竟然讓我們這把年紀仍有生命和精力去服事祂，其實是祂的厚愛和恩典。

有人感嘆人生苦短，意思是人生太短暫，轉瞬即逝，若不好好珍惜、享受當下，人生就會虛度，所以我們應該及時行樂，過自己喜歡的生活。但正如摩西所言：我們一生的年日是七十歲，若是強壯可到八十歲；但其中所矜誇的不過是勞苦愁煩，轉眼成空，我們便如飛而去。到底是甚麼令我們的人生過得更有意義？

所羅門王總結他對人生的觀察，說：你們一切都聽見了，總而言之，應當敬畏神，謹守他的誡命，因為這是每一個人的本分。人所作的每一件事，連一切隱藏的事，無論是善是惡，神都必審問。（傳道書 12:13-14）

所以，愛耶和華你們的神，行他一切的道，守他的誡命，專靠他，盡心盡性事奉他。（約書亞記 22:5）

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神讓我們仍有能力去服事祂。求神加添我們智慧和信心，讓我們懂得如何安排時間，把握機會去服事祂，更深去經歷神的愛和恩典。
  • 2. 為美國和以色列的國家領袖祈禱。求神觸動他們的心，讓他們曉得他們對國人和全世界的責任，設法早日結束戰爭。
  • 3. 為受苦節和復活節分別在各個堂會舉行的活動代禱。讓我們在復活節期間能有機會安靜反思基督復活帶給我們的盼望，讓我們能重新得力，面對人生的挑戰，完成我們在世上的使命。
  • 4. 為San Leandro分堂將於三月底開始英語崇拜的籌備工作祈禱。求神祝福，把更多要聽英語的弟兄姊妹和慕道友帶到神面前。
  • 5. 為Pleasanton的青少年事工祈禱。求神預備合適的同工來幫忙，並激勵家長能同心合意建立我們的青少年事工。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

My wife and I had a very rushed trip back to Hong Kong to visit relatives. Our schedule was so tight that we couldn’t even arrange to meet with our childhood church friends. We returned Saturday night, preached next Sunday morning. Then, I had a series of church activities, meetings, and visits during the week. Even meeting with our children and grandchildren for dinner had to be rescheduled. We were incredibly busy!

Yes, we were incredibly busy. I can imagine how much busier it would be if we had young children or elderly at home. However, my wife and I have recently shared the same feeling: we spend most of our time busy for God the Father. As full-time preachers, it is His love and grace that we, at our age, still have the life and energy to serve Him.

Some people lament that life is too short, meaning it passes in the blink of an eye. If we don’t cherish and enjoy the present moment, life will be wasted. Therefore, we should seize the day and live the life we love. But as Moses said, “The days of our years are seventy; and if by reason of strength they are eighty; but their strength is labor and sorrow; for they are soon cut off, and we fly away.” What then makes our lives more meaningful?

King Solomon summarized his observations on life, saying: “You have heard everything. Finally, fear God and keep his commandments, for this is the duty of every man. God will bring every deed into judgment, including every hidden thing, whether good or evil.” (Ecclesiastes 12:13-14)

Therefore, “Love the Lord your God and walk in all his ways and keep his commandments; trust in him and serve him with all your heart and with all your soul.” (Joshua 22:5)

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise God for enabling us to still serve Him. Let’s pray that God increase our wisdom and faith, enabling us to manage our time effectively, seize opportunities to serve Him, and experience His love and grace more deeply.
  • Pray for the national leaders of the United States and Israel. Ask God to touch their hearts, to make them aware of their responsibilities to their countrymen and the world, and to strive for an early end to the war.
  • Pray for the events to be held in various church campuses during Good Friday and Easter. May we have the opportunity during Easter to quietly reflect on the hope that Christ’s resurrection brings us, and may we be renewed in strength to face life’s challenges and fulfill our mission on earth.
  • Pray for the preparations for English worship services to begin at the San Leandro Campus at the end of March. Ask God to bless and bring more brothers and sisters in Christ and seekers who wish to worship God in English.
  • Pray for the youth ministry in Pleasanton. Ask God to provide suitable co-workers to help and to encourage parents to work together to build up our youth ministry.

posts.title: 2026-03-08-shepherdsheart
posts.excerpt: 牧人心語 我媽媽是個很固執的人。我每次回港和她傳福音，她總是敷衍我，說小時候也上過主日學，就不再理我。最近幾年，她的聽覺越來越差，又不肯戴助聽器；有一隻眼睛做了白內障手術，看東西很清楚，就拒絕為另一隻眼做手術。 我這次回港看她，她的情況更差。我弟婦告訴我，她兩隻耳都已經聽不見我們說話，但她卻樂得清靜，仍拒絕戴助聽器。我向神祈禱，我還可以怎樣和她傳福音呢？她每天除了三餐，就是睡覺，我根本沒有機會和她好好坐下來講話，就算和她講，她也聽不到。 昨天師母中午有約，我獨自一人回家探望媽媽，看見她正在吃午餐。我弟婦告訴我，她吃完午餐後會讓她坐一小時，才讓她上床休息。她吃完午餐後我就坐在她身邊，知道她有一隻眼看得很清楚，我就用手機裡的記事簿，用粗體字寫短訊和媽媽傳福音。之後我兩次邀請她信耶穌，她都點頭表示願意。 我感謝神給我這個驚喜，在我不知怎樣和媽媽傳福音，也不知有沒有機會和她分享的時候，神原來已為我預備了機會，聖靈也會指引我如何去傳，只要我們願意「專心。」 聖經說：務要傳道；無論得時不得時，總要專心。（提摩太後書 4:2）「總要專心」可譯作隨時預備或持之以恆。現代中文版的翻譯是「不管時機理想不理想都要傳。」 中東的戰爭引起世界關注，每日有很多人死亡並活在恐慌之中。就算我們不是生活在戰區，我們也感到有莫名的憂慮和壓力。這世界真的很需要聽到天國的福音，而上帝已將這任務交給了教會，也交給了每個認識和相信耶穌的人。不要擔心怎樣去傳或有沒有機會去傳，只要我們 「專心。」 招牧師 代禱事項 1. 感謝讚美神讓我們有生命氣息可以為主做見證。求神加添我們勇氣和信心，把握每個神為我們預備的機會去分享福音。 2. 為中東的戰爭早日結束祈禱。祈求神的旨意成就，叫人知道真正的和平來自認識和敬畏掌管世界的神；並求神安慰仍活在苦難中的人，賜給他們盼望。 3. 為教會的生命小組能成為栽培弟兄姊妹靈命成長和協助建立教會的平台祈禱。求神讓各生命小組的領袖和代表能鼓勵組員們能以匯點教會為家，一起完成神交給我們的使命。 4. 為教會裡正在尋找工作的弟兄姊妹們祈禱。求神加添他們信心，並在等候期間得著神所賜的平安。 5. 為教會籌備按立執事培訓班祈禱。求神賜智慧和屬靈的洞察力給教牧同工，讓他們能有效地訓練和栽培合資格的執事人選，並讓按立首批執事的計劃能順利完成。 From the Heart of a Shepherd My mother is […]
posts.content:
牧人心語

我媽媽是個很固執的人。我每次回港和她傳福音，她總是敷衍我，說小時候也上過主日學，就不再理我。最近幾年，她的聽覺越來越差，又不肯戴助聽器；有一隻眼睛做了白內障手術，看東西很清楚，就拒絕為另一隻眼做手術。

我這次回港看她，她的情況更差。我弟婦告訴我，她兩隻耳都已經聽不見我們說話，但她卻樂得清靜，仍拒絕戴助聽器。我向神祈禱，我還可以怎樣和她傳福音呢？她每天除了三餐，就是睡覺，我根本沒有機會和她好好坐下來講話，就算和她講，她也聽不到。

昨天師母中午有約，我獨自一人回家探望媽媽，看見她正在吃午餐。我弟婦告訴我，她吃完午餐後會讓她坐一小時，才讓她上床休息。她吃完午餐後我就坐在她身邊，知道她有一隻眼看得很清楚，我就用手機裡的記事簿，用粗體字寫短訊和媽媽傳福音。之後我兩次邀請她信耶穌，她都點頭表示願意。

我感謝神給我這個驚喜，在我不知怎樣和媽媽傳福音，也不知有沒有機會和她分享的時候，神原來已為我預備了機會，聖靈也會指引我如何去傳，只要我們願意「專心。」

聖經說：務要傳道；無論得時不得時，總要專心。（提摩太後書 4:2）「總要專心」可譯作隨時預備或持之以恆。現代中文版的翻譯是「不管時機理想不理想都要傳。」

中東的戰爭引起世界關注，每日有很多人死亡並活在恐慌之中。就算我們不是生活在戰區，我們也感到有莫名的憂慮和壓力。這世界真的很需要聽到天國的福音，而上帝已將這任務交給了教會，也交給了每個認識和相信耶穌的人。不要擔心怎樣去傳或有沒有機會去傳，只要我們 「專心。」

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神讓我們有生命氣息可以為主做見證。求神加添我們勇氣和信心，把握每個神為我們預備的機會去分享福音。
  • 2. 為中東的戰爭早日結束祈禱。祈求神的旨意成就，叫人知道真正的和平來自認識和敬畏掌管世界的神；並求神安慰仍活在苦難中的人，賜給他們盼望。
  • 3. 為教會的生命小組能成為栽培弟兄姊妹靈命成長和協助建立教會的平台祈禱。求神讓各生命小組的領袖和代表能鼓勵組員們能以匯點教會為家，一起完成神交給我們的使命。
  • 4. 為教會裡正在尋找工作的弟兄姊妹們祈禱。求神加添他們信心，並在等候期間得著神所賜的平安。
  • 5. 為教會籌備按立執事培訓班祈禱。求神賜智慧和屬靈的洞察力給教牧同工，讓他們能有效地訓練和栽培合資格的執事人選，並讓按立首批執事的計劃能順利完成。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

My mother is a very stubborn person. Every time I go back to Hong Kong to share the gospel with her, she always brushes me off, saying she went to Sunday school when she was young, and then ignores me. In recent years, her hearing has deteriorated, and she refuses to wear a hearing aid;.She had cataract surgery on one eye and can see clearly now, but she refuses to have surgery on the other eye.

When I visited her this time, her condition was even worse. My sister-in-law told me that she can no longer hear us talking in either ear, but she prefers peace and quiet and still refuses to wear a hearing aid. I prayed to God, how can I share the gospel with her? Besides meals, she sleeps all day; I have no chance to sit down and talk to her properly, and even if I do, she can’t hear me.

Yesterday, my wife had an appointment at noon, so I went home alone to visit my mother and saw her eating lunch. My sister-in-law told me that after lunch, she would let her sit for an hour before letting her go to bed. After she finished lunch, I sat down next to her. Knowing she had excellent vision in one eye, I used my phone’s notepad to write her a text message in bold to share the gospel with her. Afterward, I invited her to believe in Jesus twice, and she nodded in agreement both times.

I thank God for this surprise. When I didn’t know how to share the gospel with my mother, or even if I would have the opportunity to share it with her. Yet God had already prepared an opportunity for me. The Holy Spirit also guided me on how to share, because this matter has been in my heart.

The Bible says: “Preach the word; be prepared in season and out of season, be attentive.” (2 Timothy 4:2) “Be attentive” can be translated as “always be prepared” or “persistently.” The modern Chinese translation is “Preach regardless of whether the time is ideal or not.”

The war in the Middle East has drawn global attention, with many people dying daily and living in fear. Even if we don’t live in a war zone, we feel inexplicable anxiety and pressure. This world truly needs to hear the gospel of the Kingdom of Heaven, and God has entrusted this task to the church and to everyone who knows and believes in Jesus. Don’t worry about how to spread the word or whether there’s an opportunity to spread it, as long as we have the heart to share it.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise God for giving us the life and breath to bear witness for Him. May God add courage and faith so that we may seize every opportunity He has prepared for us to share the Gospel.
  • Pray for an early end to the war in the Middle East. Pray that God’s will be done, that people may know that true peace comes from knowing and fearing the God who rules the world; and pray that God comforts those still living in suffering and gives them hope.
  • Pray that the church’s life groups may become a platform for nurturing the spiritual growth of brothers and sisters and assisting in building the church. May God enable the leaders and representatives of each life group to encourage their members to consider Crosspoint Church their spiritual home and work together to fulfill the mission God has given us.
  • Pray for the brothers and sisters in the church who are currently seeking employment. May God increase their faith and grant them the peace while they wait.
  • Pray for the church to prepare a training course for potential deacon candidates. Let us pray that God will grant wisdom and spiritual insight to our pastoral staff so that they may effectively train and cultivate qualified deacon candidates, and also pray that the plan to ordain the first batch of deacons may be successfully completed.

posts.title: 2026-03-01-shepherdsheart
posts.excerpt: 牧人心語 我是個停不下來的人。無論是帶領教會或在家裡，總希望把教會和家裡的事務做好，把每個人都照顧得妥妥當當。但年紀大了，事務多了，總會有疏忽的時候和遺漏的地方。最近師母就提醒我要早點回電郵，讓人知道我已收到他們的資訊。 今早讀聖經時讀到（詩篇 73），其中有句說話十分受用，也是很大的提醒。詩人說： 我的肉體和我的心腸衰殘；但神是我心裡的力量，又是我的福分，直到永遠。（詩篇 73:26）和合 是的，我的肉體和我的心腸衰殘。我真的感覺有點累了，不單是身體感到疲憊，心也有點累了。但感謝讚美神，神是我心裡的力量。每當我的身體、心靈感到困乏時，祂總會用祂的說話或周邊的人和事賜我力量，讓我可以繼續振翅高飛。 現代中文版把『又是我的福分，直到永遠』翻成『永遠是我所需要的一切！』是的，當我在疲乏時意識到神同在，將我心中的憂慮和所掛念的事交給祂，我就能用平常心去面對一切。這就是我所需要的一切！ 主內的弟兄姊妹，神就是我們所需要的一切。問題是當我們有需要時，我們有沒有意識到祂的同在。這是祂給所有相信祂的人的應許，祂常與我們同在。我們感受不到是因為我們被眼前的危機和困境捆綁，又被心中的憂慮和恐懼籠罩，所以忘記了神就在我們身邊。 詩人說：你們要休息，要知道我是神！我必在外邦中被尊崇，在遍地上也被尊崇。（詩篇 46:10）和合 現代中文版的翻譯是：『要停止戰爭，承認我是上帝。』是的，我們要停下來，我們要停止靠自己去爭戰、去面對和處理眼前的困境和危機。當我們能停下來去尋找上帝，就發覺祂其實離我們不遠，就在我們身邊。有神做我們的後盾，有誰能敵擋我們呢？ 所以，主內的弟兄姊妹，如果你發覺自己很難靜下來，可以試試來參加在Milpitas母堂每逢第二及第四 8:15am 舉行的『呼吸祈禱會』，或可在自己的堂點開始一個類似的祈禱會，學習在呼吸中安靜、聆聽神的聲音，幫助我們能常常意識到神的同在。 招牧師 代禱事項 1. 感謝讚美神讓賜給我們祂常與我們同在的應許。求神幫助我們不再自己掙扎尋求解決眼前困境的方法，轉而尋求神的引領和幫助。 2. 感謝讚美神讓我們可以在世上成為祂的證人，讓人知道祂是神。祂應許的天國近了，求神激勵我們要把握機會將這福音帶給更多人。 3. 為李業基牧師、師母交通意外沒有受傷感謝讚美神。求神讓他們患難中更深體驗到神對他們的愛及弟兄姊妹的關心。 4. 為教會弟兄姊妹的屬靈生命能有新突破，繼續成長祈禱。求神裝備我們成為祂一支能為祂在末世爭戰，可以搶救更多失喪靈魂的強大隊伍。 5. 為考慮接受執事培訓的弟兄姊妹們祈禱。求神讓他們能明白神的心意，並願意接受培訓，進一步了解作為教會執事的位份與職責，敢於回應神給他們的呼召。 From the Heart […]
posts.content:
牧人心語

我是個停不下來的人。無論是帶領教會或在家裡，總希望把教會和家裡的事務做好，把每個人都照顧得妥妥當當。但年紀大了，事務多了，總會有疏忽的時候和遺漏的地方。最近師母就提醒我要早點回電郵，讓人知道我已收到他們的資訊。

今早讀聖經時讀到（詩篇 73），其中有句說話十分受用，也是很大的提醒。詩人說：

我的肉體和我的心腸衰殘；但神是我心裡的力量，又是我的福分，直到永遠。（詩篇 73:26）和合

是的，我的肉體和我的心腸衰殘。我真的感覺有點累了，不單是身體感到疲憊，心也有點累了。但感謝讚美神，神是我心裡的力量。每當我的身體、心靈感到困乏時，祂總會用祂的說話或周邊的人和事賜我力量，讓我可以繼續振翅高飛。

現代中文版把『又是我的福分，直到永遠』翻成『永遠是我所需要的一切！』是的，當我在疲乏時意識到神同在，將我心中的憂慮和所掛念的事交給祂，我就能用平常心去面對一切。這就是我所需要的一切！

主內的弟兄姊妹，神就是我們所需要的一切。問題是當我們有需要時，我們有沒有意識到祂的同在。這是祂給所有相信祂的人的應許，祂常與我們同在。我們感受不到是因為我們被眼前的危機和困境捆綁，又被心中的憂慮和恐懼籠罩，所以忘記了神就在我們身邊。

詩人說：你們要休息，要知道我是神！我必在外邦中被尊崇，在遍地上也被尊崇。（詩篇 46:10）和合

現代中文版的翻譯是：『要停止戰爭，承認我是上帝。』是的，我們要停下來，我們要停止靠自己去爭戰、去面對和處理眼前的困境和危機。當我們能停下來去尋找上帝，就發覺祂其實離我們不遠，就在我們身邊。有神做我們的後盾，有誰能敵擋我們呢？

所以，主內的弟兄姊妹，如果你發覺自己很難靜下來，可以試試來參加在Milpitas母堂每逢第二及第四 8:15am 舉行的『呼吸祈禱會』，或可在自己的堂點開始一個類似的祈禱會，學習在呼吸中安靜、聆聽神的聲音，幫助我們能常常意識到神的同在。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 1. 感謝讚美神讓賜給我們祂常與我們同在的應許。求神幫助我們不再自己掙扎尋求解決眼前困境的方法，轉而尋求神的引領和幫助。
  • 2. 感謝讚美神讓我們可以在世上成為祂的證人，讓人知道祂是神。祂應許的天國近了，求神激勵我們要把握機會將這福音帶給更多人。
  • 3. 為李業基牧師、師母交通意外沒有受傷感謝讚美神。求神讓他們患難中更深體驗到神對他們的愛及弟兄姊妹的關心。
  • 4. 為教會弟兄姊妹的屬靈生命能有新突破，繼續成長祈禱。求神裝備我們成為祂一支能為祂在末世爭戰，可以搶救更多失喪靈魂的強大隊伍。
  • 5. 為考慮接受執事培訓的弟兄姊妹們祈禱。求神讓他們能明白神的心意，並願意接受培訓，進一步了解作為教會執事的位份與職責，敢於回應神給他們的呼召。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

I am a person who can’t stop. Whether leading the church or at home, I always hope to do a good job in church and home, taking good care of everyone. But as I get older and have more responsibilities, there are bound to be times when I neglect things and overlook things. Recently, my wife reminded me to reply to emails earlier so that people know I’ve received their messages.

This morning, while reading the Bible, I came across Psalm 73, and one verse in particular was very helpful and a great reminder to me. The psalmist said:

“My flesh and my heart are wasting away, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.” (Psalm 73:26)

Yes, my flesh and my heart are wasting away. I really feel a bit anxious, not just physically, but also mentally. But thank God, He is the strength of my heart. Whenever my body and soul feel weary, He always gives me strength through His words or the people and things around me, allowing me to continue to soar.

The Modern Chinese version translates “Again, my blessing, forever” as “He is all I need forever!” Yes, when I am weary and realize God’s presence, I can pass all my anxieties and worries to Him, and I can face everything with equanimity. He is all I need!

Brothers and sisters in the Lord, God is all we need. The question is, when we have needs, are we aware of His presence? This is His promise to all who believe in Him; He is always with us. We don’t feel it because we are bound by the current crisis and predicament, and shrouded in the anxieties and fears of our hearts, so we forget that God is right beside us.

The psalmist says: “Be still, and know that I am God! I will be exalted among the nations, exalted throughout the earth.” (Psalm 46:10)

The Modern Chinese version translates it as: “Stop the war, acknowledge that I am God.” Yes, we must stop; we must stop fighting, facing, and dealing with the current predicament and crisis on our own. When we stop to seek God, we discover that He is actually not far from us, but right beside us. With God as our support, who can stand against us?

Therefore, brothers and sisters in Christ, if you find it difficult to quiet your mind, try attending the “Breathing Prayer Meeting” held at the Milpitas main campus on every 2nd and 4th Sunday mornings at 8:15, or you can start a similar one at your own church campus, learning to quiet your mind with your breathing, listen to God’s voice, and help yourself to be constantly aware of God’s presence.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise God for His promise to always be with us. Let’s ask that God help us stop struggling to find solutions to our current difficulties by ourselves but instead seek His guidance and help.
  • Praise God for enabling us to be His witnesses in this world, letting others know that He is God. His promised kingdom is near; let us ask that God encourage us to seize the opportunity to bring this gospel to many more people.
  • Praise God for Pastor Gideon and his wife’s survival in the car accident. Let us ask that God made them experience His love for them and the care of the brothers and sisters in Christ during this life epistle.
  • Pray for new breakthroughs and continued growth in the spiritual lives of our brothers and sisters in the church. Let us ask that God equip us to become a powerful force capable of fighting for Him in the last days to rescue more lost souls.
  • Pray for our brothers and sisters in Christ who are considering to receive deacon training. Let us ask that God help them understand His will, and be willing to accept the training, so that they can understand the position and responsibilities as church deacons, and dare to respond to God’s call.

posts.content:
牧人心語

上星期日，得悉一個主內弟兄在農曆新年前回天家了。師母在去年聖誕節還和他通電話，知道他身體欠佳，但想不到他已不辭而別。先兩天我和師母聯絡上他的妻子，特別去探望她和安慰她，才曉得他不想麻煩我去探望他。他返天家的時間因年近歲晚，所以他妻子也不想在這個時候通知我和師母這個消息，他走得很安詳，並已安葬。

我和師母告訴他的妻子，教會的弟兄姊妹都很關心他們，她應該早點告訴我們他的健康狀況惡化，讓我們可以去探望他，和他一起唱詩祈禱。但他們夫婦太為他人設想，不想麻煩弟兄姊妹。豈不知神在地上設立教會，除了傳福音外，就是要我們在這充滿苦難的世界中互相照顧，彼此幫助，讓我們得安慰。

我們在世上有苦難，2026年一開始就動亂頻繁，我們越來越難掌握明天。但我們若有一個屬靈的家，我們就是落在患難中也會主內的弟兄姊妹幫助，並且讓我們可以更新使命，將真正的平安帶給人。

主內的弟兄姊妹，我希望你能把匯點教會作為你屬靈的家。當你有需要，不要客氣回家尋求幫助。我也鼓勵你若有能力，大家一起建立這個家，讓它健壯起來，能幫助更多人找到真正的平安和盼望。

下星期六，三月七日，北加州華人浸信會區聯會將在San Leandro分堂舉行領袖訓練大會。主題為一團隊，一夢想。請大家上網或向小組代表報名參加，一起學習建立有效的事奉團隊，讓我們能繼續為神作大事。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 感謝讚美神讓夫婦營和青少年冬令會完滿結束。參加的弟兄姊妹和青少年們都獲益良多。求神保守和賜福參與者，讓夫妻的生活更美滿，青少年的屬靈生命不斷成長。
  • 感謝讚美神賜給我們一個屬靈的家。求神讓我們更深體會弟兄姊妹間的愛，一起建立和鞏固神的家。
  • 為三月七日的領袖訓練大會祈禱。求聖靈感動各教會的弟兄姊妹一齊來做夢，團結合作完成教會使命。
  • 為星期五晚在Milpitas 母堂舉行的英語敬拜祈禱聚會代禱。求神讓參加者能進一步藉敬拜、分享見證和互相代求彼此激勵，追求靈命的進深和突破。
  • 為執事按立籌備委員會邀請考慮接受執事培訓的弟兄姊妹祈禱。求神的引領他們明白神的心意，並憑信心回應聖靈的感動。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

Last Sunday, I learned that a brother in Christ had passed away before the Lunar New Year. My wife had spoken with him on the phone last Christmas and learned that he was unwell, but we could not believe he would leave us so soon. Two days ago, my wife and I visited his wife and comforted her. We found out that this brother in Christ did not want to bother us because he knew I was pretty busy. His wife did not notify us about his passing because it was near the Chinese lunar New Year. She told us that her husband passed away peacefully and has been buried.

My wife and I told his wife that the brothers and sisters in the church cared deeply for them. She should have told us earlier about his deteriorating health so we could have visited him, sang hymns, and prayed with him. But they were too considerate of others and didn’t want to trouble the brothers and sisters. Little did they know that God established the church on earth not only to spread the gospel but also to care for and help one another in this world full of suffering. God prepared a place for us to find comfort in each other: the church.

We face suffering every day; 2026 has begun with frequent unrest, and we find it increasingly difficult to predict tomorrow. But if we have a spiritual home, even in times of trouble, our brothers and sisters in Christ will help us, and we can renew our mission to bring true peace to people in times like this.

Brothers and sisters in Christ, I hope you will consider Crosspoint Church your spiritual home. When you need help, don’t hesitate to come home for assistance. I also encourage you, if you are able and willing, to work together to build up this home, making it healthy so that it can help more people find true peace and hope in Christ.

Next Saturday, March 7th, the Northern California Chinese Baptist Association will hold a leadership conference at our San Leandro campus. The theme is “One Team, One Dream.” Please register online () or with your life group representative so that we can learn to build an effective ministry team to do great things for God.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Praise God for the successful conclusion of the Marriage Camp and the Youth Winter Retreat. All the participating brothers and sisters and the youth benefited greatly. May God protect and bless the participants, making the lives of couples more fulfilling and the spiritual lives of the youth continuously growing.
  • Praise God for giving us a spiritual home. May God help us experience more deeply the love among brothers and sisters, and to build and strengthen God’s home together.
  • Pray for the Leadership Conference on March 7th. May the Holy Spirit move brothers and sisters from all churches to dream together, unite and cooperate to fulfill their church’s mission.
  • Pray for the English worship and prayer meeting to be held at the Milpitas Main Campus on Friday evening. May God enable the participants to further encourage one another through worship and prayer, sharing testimonies, and mutual intercession, pursuing spiritual growth and breakthroughs.
  • Pray for the brothers and sisters invited by the Deacon Ordination Planning Committee to consider receiving deacon training. May God guide them to understand His will and respond to the promptings of the Holy Spirit with faith.

posts.title: 2026-02-15-shepherdsheart

posts.content:
牧人心語

上星期日早上八點鐘，我參加了在Milpitas母堂舉行的靜默祈禱會，粵語崇拜後到半島福音堂參加由Belmont City主辦的農曆新年社區活動。發覺有很多年輕家庭帶同孩子出席該活動，講粵語的也有好幾十人，我深感那裡是個可以收割的屬靈禾場。星期一，我和半島福音堂的核心同工們分析和討論未來發展的路向，獲得共識，並一起為福音堂祈禱，求神預備工人和其它資源。

我和師母在過去幾天到不同堂會開會和參加活動，看見弟兄姊妹把各個堂會佈置得美輪美奐，增添了不少過農曆年的氣氛。我知道在這個週末有很多小組舉行慶祝農曆新年活動，我在此預祝大家新春快樂，恩典滿滿。

我們的青少年人將於二月廿七日，星期五晚在Milpitas母堂舉行分享祈禱會，以英語進行，歡迎各堂會的弟兄姊妹出席參加，聆聽年青人的分享並為教會祈禱。

北加州華人浸信會區聯會將於三月七日星期六早上九時半至三時舉行領袖訓練大會。主題為一團隊，一夢想。希望能幫助各教會的事奉團隊同心服事，建設神的家。請各弟兄姊妹向堂主任查詢詳情，一起報名參加。

我親愛的弟兄們，你們務要堅固，不可搖動，常常竭力多做主工；因為知道，你們的勞苦在主裡面不是徒然的。（歌林多前書 15:58）

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 感謝讚美神的厚恩。祂讓我們在動蕰不安的世代中仍充滿盼望，享受生活的豐盛。
  • 為參加冬令退修會的青少年人祈禱。求神在退修會中激發年青人愛神愛人的心，讓他們能被神使用，成為使人蒙福的渠道。
  • 為半島福音堂的未來發展祈禱。求神開路，並讓核心同工們能同感一靈，在神的帶領下引領更多當地人士認識神和天國福音。
  • 為San Leandro分堂正在籌備開始英語崇拜祈禱。求神加添智慧和人力資源，讓我們能成功拓展下一代的事工。
  • 為教牧同工們的身體、心靈和靈性健康祈禱。求神保守他們事奉神的熱忱，並能以基督犧牲的愛牧養會眾。

 

From the Heart of a Shepherd

Last Sunday morning at 8:00 AM, I attended a silent prayer meeting at the Milpitas main campus. Then, after preaching at the Cantonese worship service, I went to the Peninsula Mission Church for a Lunar New Year community event hosted by the City of Belmont. I noticed many young families with their children attending, and at least dozens of people spoke Cantonese. I felt it was a spiritually fertile ground. On Monday, the core members of the Peninsula Mission Church met at my house and we analyzed and discussed the future direction of the church. We reached a consensus, and prayed together, asking God to prepare workers and other resources to help develop the mission church.

My wife and I visited various church campuses and attended activities over the past few days. We saw brothers and sisters decorating each church campus beautifully, adding to the Lunar New Year a joyful atmosphere. I know many life groups are holding Lunar New Year celebrations this weekend. I wish everyone a happy and blessed Year ahead.

Our youth group will hold a praise and prayer meeting on Friday evening, February 27th, at the Milpitas main campus. It will be conducted in English. Brothers and sisters from all churches are welcome to attend, listen to the young people’s sharing, and pray for the church together.

The Northern California Chinese Baptist Association will hold a leadership training conference on Saturday, March 7th, from 9:30 AM to 3:00 PM. The theme is “One Team, One Dream.” The purpose is to help church ministry teams serve together in unity and build up God’s house. Brothers and sisters, please contact your campus pastor for details and register to attend.

My dear brothers and sisters, steadfast and immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that your labor in the Lord is not in vain. (1 Corinthians 15:58).

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • We give thanks and praise to God for His abundant grace. He fills us with hope and allows us to enjoy the abundance of life even in turbulent times.
  • Pray for the youth attending the winter retreat. May God inspire love for God and man in them, enabling them to be used by God and become channels of God’s blessing.
  • Pray for the future development of the Peninsula Mission Church. May God pave the way and enable our core members to be of one spirit, leading more local people to know God and the Gospel of Heaven under His guidance.
  • Pray for the San Leandro campus as it prepares to begin English worship services. May God add wisdom and man power so that we can successfully expand our ministry to the next generation.
  • Pray for the physical, mental, and spiritual health of our pastoral staff. May God preserve their zeal for serving God and enable them to shepherd the congregation with the sacrificial love of Christ.

posts.title: 2026-02-08-shepherdsheart

posts.content:
牧人心語

我們教牧同工們於上星期三開會檢討教會事工進展。發覺今年的情人節與農曆新年十分接近，上半年全教會性的活動也頗多，所以決定取消原定在二月十四日舉行的婚姻講座，並將家庭門徒訓練運動延後舉行。

下個星期日，二月十五日，是農曆年廿八，我們各個堂會都有慶祝活動，迎接馬年的來臨。在這個動盪不安、令人擔憂的世代，讓我們藉著這些活動帶給親友們點點歡樂，並把握機會和他們分享我們在基督耶穌裡的平安和盼望。

耶穌說：在世上，你們有苦難；但你們可以放心，我已經勝了世界。（約翰福音 16: 33）

耶穌告訴門徒會遭遇苦難，甚至因恐懼離他而去，剩下他一個人。但他們毋須恐慌，或為他擔憂，因為有天父與他同在。耶穌將這些事告訴他們，是要叫他們放心。

門徒會因為耶穌這樣對他們說就可以放心嗎？我認為很難。但門徒不單只是聽到耶穌這樣對他們說，門徒更從耶穌講這些話的時候，感受到耶穌掌控萬事、包括勝過苦難的把握。因為耶穌知道並深信天父與他同在。有天父上帝與他同在，他還有甚麼可懼怕的呢？不單如此，耶穌對門徒說：我已經勝了世界。耶穌是說，他已經勝了世界，並不是說將會勝過世界。我相信這個滿有把握的印象深印在門徒腦海中，給他們有極大的鼓勵。而聖靈藉約翰將他們這個經驗記載在聖經裡，是要帶給我們提醒和激勵。

耶穌說：我將這些事告訴你們，是要叫你們在我裡面有平安。

我們三個分堂的青少年人將於這個週末參加教會舉辦的冬令退修會，並計劃於二月二十七日星期五晚，在Milpitas主堂舉行英語聯合敬拜見證祈禱會。鼓勵弟兄姊妹攜同年青人一起出席參加。

除此之外，Milpitas主堂將從三月四日晚開始，舉行有一連六次的實體弟兄查經班，查考雅各書，時間為晚上八時至九時卅分。鼓勵主內的弟兄們盡快報名出席參加。

我們教會的求職推薦平台已開始運作，歡迎有需要的弟兄姊妹利用這個平台與教會內其他在職的弟兄姊妹聯繫。互相幫忙、彼此激勵。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 感謝神應許將平安賜給我們。求聖靈提醒我們要將一切憂慮卸給神，深信神的保守和看顧。耶穌已經勝了世界。
  • 為各堂會農曆新年的慶祝和外展活動祈禱。求神將喜樂平安加給我們，並藉此能邀請更多親友與教會的弟兄姊妹認識。
  • 為教會各堂會的小組事工代禱。求神加添牧者和小組代表們的愛心和屬靈智慧，能有效地支持和鼓勵參加小組的弟兄姊妹追求屬靈生命的成長，並同心幫忙建立神的家。
  • 為教會正積極聘請能幫助教會發展的牧者祈禱。求神憐憫，早日差派合適的工人到我們當中，與現有的教牧一起建立基督的身體。
  • 為正在求職和想轉工的主內弟兄姊妹祈禱。求神賜平安，並早日將合適的工作賜給他們，並能在工作上見證神。

 

From the Heart of a Shepherd

Our pastoral staff met last Wednesday to review the progress of church ministry. We realized that Valentine’s Day and the Lunar New Year are very close this year, and there have been many church-wide activities in the first half of the year. Therefore, we decided to cancel the marriage enrichment seminar originally scheduled for February 14th and postpone the family discipleship campaign.

Next Sunday, February 15th, is two-day from the Lunar New Year. All our church campuses will have celebrations to welcome the Year of the Horse. In this turbulent and worrying generation, let us use these activities to bring joy to our loved ones and seize the opportunity to share with them the peace and hope we have in Christ Jesus.

Jesus said: “In this world you will have tribulations. But take heart! I have overcome the world.” (John 16:33)

Jesus told his disciples that they would have tribulations, and would abandon Jesus in fear, leaving Jesus alone. But they did not need to panic or worry about him at all, because the Father was with him. Jesus told them these things to give them peace of mind.

Could the disciples have felt reassured simply because Jesus said this to them? I think it’s hard. But the disciples didn’t just hear Jesus say this; they sensed from his words a sense of in control over all things, including overcoming suffering. This was because Jesus knew and deeply believed in the Father’s presence. With God the Father with him, what had he to fear? Furthermore, Jesus told his disciples, “I have overcome the world.” Jesus meant that he had already overcome the world, not that he would overcome it. I believe Jesus’ sense of assurance was deeply imprinted in the disciples’ minds, giving them immense encouragement. The Holy Spirit recorded this experience in the Bible through John to remind and inspire us today.

Jesus said, “I have told you these things, so that in me you may have peace.”

Our youth from 3 church campuses will be attending a winter’s retreat this weekend and plan to hold an English joint worship and prayer meeting on Friday evening, February 27th, at the Milpitas main campus. Brothers and sisters are encouraged to bring your young people to join them.

In addition, Milpitas Campus will be holding a six-week in-person man’s Bible study class, starting on March 4th, from 8:00 PM to 9:30 PM. The class will study the Book of James. Brothers in Christ are encouraged to sign-up and join the class.

Our church’s online job referral platform is now operational. Brothers and sisters in need are welcome to use this platform to connect with other working brothers and sisters in the church. Let’s help and encourage one another.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Thank God for His promise to give us peace. Ask the Holy Spirit to remind us to cast all our anxieties on Him, and to have deep faith in His protection and care. Jesus has overcome the world.
  • Pray for the Lunar New Year celebrations and outreach activities of all church campuses. Ask God to add joy and peace to our lives, and use these activities to invite our relatives and friends to meet with our brothers and sisters in the church.
  • Pray for the life group ministries. Ask God to add love and spiritual wisdom to our pastors and life group representatives, so that they can effectively support and encourage the brothers and sisters participating in the life groups to pursue spiritual growth and work together to build up the church.
  • Pray for the church’s active recruitment of pastors who can help the church develop. Ask God to have mercy upon us and send suitable workers to us soon, so that the body of Christ can be built up.
  • Pray for brothers and sisters in Christ who are seeking jobs or looking to change jobs. Ask God to grant them peace and to give them suitable jobs soon, so that they can witness God’s goodness with their work.

posts.content:
牧人心語

這星期我收到兩個壞消息。一個是我的小學同學突然昏迷送院，現在仍未甦醒。另一個是得悉教會有兩個姊妹患上癌症，情況十分嚴峻。

人會病會死，其實是個常態。問題是疾病與死亡會帶給人不安和恐懼。對基督徒而言，我們同樣會因疾病與死亡的臨近感到恐懼和憂傷，耶穌在客西馬尼園時，也極其憂傷，甚至到死。但基督徒有全能神作為倚靠，並知道自己的生命是在愛我們的神手中，這個確信可以帶給我們出人意外的平安。

我們基督徒都會病、會死，但我們相信，我們在地上的生命只是永恆生命的起點。我們在地上的生活，是與永恆連上關係。我們要明白，永恆上帝是要用我們生活的種種，包括疾病與死亡的威脅，來鍛鍊和準備我們進入永恆裡生活。這個認知，完全改變了我們面對疾病與死亡的態度。

基督徒在面對疾病與死亡時，不是不懼怕，而是在懼怕中知道有神看顧、有主內的弟兄姊妹關心；我們不是因為不會死，而是因為死亡已被復活的主跨越。死亡不是一個句點，而是一個逗號。耶穌應許我們，信祂的人永遠不死。

所以，我們不喪膽。外體雖然毀壞，內心卻一天新似一天。我們這至暫至輕的苦楚，要為我們成就極重無比、永遠的榮耀。（歌林多後書 4: 16-17）

親愛的天父上帝，我要感謝讚美你，因你常與我們同在，讓我們感到懼怕憂傷時可以倚靠你，從你那裡得安慰和力量。天父上帝，求你將你所應許的平安賜給你的女兒們，我們所愛的姊妹，也求你賜恩典和能力給我同學的家人，和這兩個姊妹的家人。阿們。

我教會的同工預備了兩個連線，是一個求職弟兄姊妹尋找工作的網絡平台，有需要的弟兄姊妹可利用這個平台與教會內其他在職弟兄姊妹聯繫。

http://crosspointchurchsv.org/jobreferral/

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 感謝神提醒我們祂的同在。求聖靈經常觸動我們的心，讓我們每日都感受到神的保守和看顧。
  • 為各堂會的小組正密鑼緊鼓地籌備農曆新年的慶祝和外展活動祈禱。求神加添我們主內一家的歡樂，並藉此能與未信主的家人、朋友分享神的祝福。
  • 為即將來臨的青少年冬令會祈禱。我們已有四十多人報名參加這營會，請為導師們和冬令會的講員祈禱。求神使用他們堅固參加者的信心和屬靈生命，讓他們渴慕認識神的心不斷增長。
  • 為教會正物識和邀請教牧同工參加匯點的教牧團隊祈禱。求神差遣合適的工人到我們當中。
  • 為半島分堂於二月八日參加Belmont City 舉辦的農曆新年活動祈禱。求神藉此幫助該分堂的弟兄姊妹能與更多當地的華人認識。

From the Heart of a Shepherd

This week I received two bad news. One was that my elementary school classmate suddenly fell into a coma and was hospitalized; he is still unconscious. The other was that I learned two sisters in our church were diagnosed with cancer, and their conditions are very serious.

Getting sick and dying is a normal part of life. The problem is that illness and death bring anxiety and fear. As Christians, we also experience fear and sorrow at the approach of illness and death. Jesus was deeply sorrowful in the Garden of Gethsemane, even to the point of death. But Christians have Almighty God as their reliance, and the certainty that our lives are in the hands of a God who loves us brings us a peace that surpasses all understanding.

We Christians will all get sick and die, but we believe that our life on earth is merely the beginning of eternal life. Our life on earth is connected to eternity. We must understand that the eternal God uses all aspects of our lives, including the threat of illness and death, to train and prepare us for life in eternity. This understanding completely changes our attitude towards illness and death.

When Christians face illness and death, it is not that they are not afraid, but that they know God cares for them and their brothers and sisters in the Lord are concerned for them. We are not immortal because we are invincible, but because death has been overcome by the resurrected Lord Jesus. Death is not a period, but a comma. Jesus promises us that those who believe in Him will never die.

Therefore, we do not lose heart. Though outwardly we are wasting away, yet inwardly we are being renewed day by day. For our light and momentary troubles are achieving for us an eternal glory that far outweighs them all. (2 Corinthians 4:16-17)

Dear Heavenly Father, I thank and praise You, for You are always with us, allowing us to rely on You in times of fear and sorrow, receiving comfort and strength from You. Heavenly Father, I ask that You grant the peace You have promised to Your daughters, our beloved sisters, and also that You grant grace and strength to the families of my classmate and the families of these two sisters. Amen.

Our church staff has prepared an online platform to help job-seeking brothers and sisters to find employment. For those who are looking for jobs, the platform will you connect with other employed brothers and sisters in the church.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • Thank God for reminding us of His presence. May the Holy Spirit continually touch our hearts, so that we may feel God’s protection and care daily.
  • Pray for the life groups in our church campuses as they are busily preparing for the Lunar New Year celebrations and outreach activities. May God increase the joy of our family in Christ, and through this, share God’s blessings with unbelieving family and friends.
  • Pray for the upcoming youth winter camp. We have already registered over forty people for this camp. Please pray for the mentors and speakers at the winter camp. May God use them to strengthen the faith and spiritual lives of the participants, and let their thirst for knowing God grow ever stronger.
  • Pray that the church is interviewing and inviting pastors and ministers to join the Crosspoint pastoral team. May God send suitable workers to join the ministry.
  • Pray for the Peninsula mission church as they signed up to participate the Lunar New Year event hosted by Belmont City on February 8th. May God help the brothers and sisters over there to get to know more local Chinese people through this opportunity.

posts.title: 2026-01-25-shepherdsheart
posts.excerpt: 牧人心語 星期三，我和師母跟一個從前的會員午餐。這個姊妹沒有帶先生來，因她想我和師母能幫她解開心中的鬱悶。她需要很小心照顧丈夫，因他的柏金遜病情愈來愈嚴重，但最令她憂心的是她的兒子。她兒子四十歲了，一直找不到理想的工作，沒辦法只好搬回家與他們一起住。所以她憂心忡忡，愁容滿面。 我相信她不是唯一面對類似處境的人。我已聽說就是在我們教會裡面，也有人被解僱後找不到工作的傳聞。事實上，雖然美國的經濟數據似乎穩定，失業率也從去年十一月的 4.5% 下調至十二月的 4.4%，但失業和兼職的在職者仍有 8.4%（十一月是 8.7%）。這些數據反映出，可能接近有 10% 的在職者和4.4%的失業人事正面工作上帶給他們的憂慮和壓力，這當然也會影響他們的家人。 我們生活在這裡的基督徒同樣需要面對以上的處境，但不一樣的地方，是我們在基督裡對這些壓力和磨難有不同的看法。聖經說：萬事都互相效力，叫愛神的人得益處。（羅馬書 8:28）這『萬事』當然也包括我們看見的好事和壞事、我們所遇見的順境和逆境。如果我們相信神的話，我們就有一個不一樣的態度去面對『萬事。』 我們若愛神，我們就要信得過，神讓『萬事』發生在我們身上是有祂的美意。我們若能看得通，明白神要我們學些甚麼功課，或經歷祂的應許和幫助，就能從中得著益處，並且會從新得力去面對人生種種挑戰。 教會目前正計畫建立一個職場推薦網絡，旨在連結正在求職的與在職的匯點人，期望透過在職者所屬公司的內部推薦機制(internal referral)，為求職者提供工作機會。詳情很快公布。 此外，弟兄姊妹如欲提名合資格的弟兄姊妹接受執事候選人培訓，請資詢被提名人同意，連同和議人簽名於一月卅一日前郵寄或送交教會辦事處，信封上請註明給按立執事籌備委員會啟。 招牧師 代禱事項 感謝神讓我們各個堂點的事工在新一年裡仍繼續展開，並有更多弟兄姊妹願意起來服事。求神幫助教牧同工們能帶領會眾完成神賜給我們的使命。 為正在尋找工作或想轉工的弟兄姊妹祈禱。求神指引他們在尋找過程中能順從聖靈的引導，並在與神對話中慢慢能體會神的心意，活在神的愛中。 為教會各堂點正在籌備農暦新年的慶祝和外展活動祈禱。求神讓弟兄姊妹在籌備過程中能享受與神與人同工的喜樂，並求神祝福和使用這些活動。 求神感動合資格成為執事候選人的弟兄姊妹，讓他們有樂意服事神的心和謙卑的態度，被提名時願意回應神的呼召。 為教牧同工和職員祈禱。他們需要承擔加倍的工作，以應付人手短缺的情況。也求神差派合適的同工早日參與教會團隊的服事。 奉獻簡報 十二月常費奉獻 $ 695,801.64 一月常費奉獻 $ 97,625.14 […]
posts.content:
牧人心語

星期三，我和師母跟一個從前的會員午餐。這個姊妹沒有帶先生來，因她想我和師母能幫她解開心中的鬱悶。她需要很小心照顧丈夫，因他的柏金遜病情愈來愈嚴重，但最令她憂心的是她的兒子。她兒子四十歲了，一直找不到理想的工作，沒辦法只好搬回家與他們一起住。所以她憂心忡忡，愁容滿面。

我相信她不是唯一面對類似處境的人。我已聽說就是在我們教會裡面，也有人被解僱後找不到工作的傳聞。事實上，雖然美國的經濟數據似乎穩定，失業率也從去年十一月的 4.5% 下調至十二月的 4.4%，但失業和兼職的在職者仍有 8.4%（十一月是 8.7%）。這些數據反映出，可能接近有 10% 的在職者和4.4%的失業人事正面工作上帶給他們的憂慮和壓力，這當然也會影響他們的家人。

我們生活在這裡的基督徒同樣需要面對以上的處境，但不一樣的地方，是我們在基督裡對這些壓力和磨難有不同的看法。聖經說：萬事都互相效力，叫愛神的人得益處。（羅馬書 8:28）這『萬事』當然也包括我們看見的好事和壞事、我們所遇見的順境和逆境。如果我們相信神的話，我們就有一個不一樣的態度去面對『萬事。』

我們若愛神，我們就要信得過，神讓『萬事』發生在我們身上是有祂的美意。我們若能看得通，明白神要我們學些甚麼功課，或經歷祂的應許和幫助，就能從中得著益處，並且會從新得力去面對人生種種挑戰。

教會目前正計畫建立一個職場推薦網絡，旨在連結正在求職的與在職的匯點人，期望透過在職者所屬公司的內部推薦機制(internal referral)，為求職者提供工作機會。詳情很快公布。

此外，弟兄姊妹如欲提名合資格的弟兄姊妹接受執事候選人培訓，請資詢被提名人同意，連同和議人簽名於一月卅一日前郵寄或送交教會辦事處，信封上請註明給按立執事籌備委員會啟。

招牧師

代禱事項

  • 感謝神讓我們各個堂點的事工在新一年裡仍繼續展開，並有更多弟兄姊妹願意起來服事。求神幫助教牧同工們能帶領會眾完成神賜給我們的使命。
  • 為正在尋找工作或想轉工的弟兄姊妹祈禱。求神指引他們在尋找過程中能順從聖靈的引導，並在與神對話中慢慢能體會神的心意，活在神的愛中。
  • 為教會各堂點正在籌備農暦新年的慶祝和外展活動祈禱。求神讓弟兄姊妹在籌備過程中能享受與神與人同工的喜樂，並求神祝福和使用這些活動。
  • 求神感動合資格成為執事候選人的弟兄姊妹，讓他們有樂意服事神的心和謙卑的態度，被提名時願意回應神的呼召。
  • 為教牧同工和職員祈禱。他們需要承擔加倍的工作，以應付人手短缺的情況。也求神差派合適的同工早日參與教會團隊的服事。

奉獻簡報

十二月常費奉獻 $ 695,801.64
一月常費奉獻 $ 97,625.14
十二月差傳奉獻 $ 13,431.99
一月差傳奉獻 $ 1,355.17
十二月社區中心奉獻 $ 3,287.85
一月社區中心奉獻 $ 1,500.00

財政簡報

一月至十一月總收入： $ 2,658,214.00
一月至十一月總開支： $ 3,056,803.20
一月至十一月缺欠： $ 398,589.20

From the Heart of a Shepherd

On Wednesday, my wife and I had lunch with a former member. This sister didn’t bring her husband because she wanted us to help ease her distress. She needs to take good care of her husband as his Parkinson’s disease worsens, but what worries her most is her son. Her son is forty years old and hasn’t been able to find a good job, so he had to move back home with them. She was very worried and looked distressed.

I believe she is not the only one facing a similar situation. I’ve heard rumors even within our church about people being laid off and unable to find a job. In fact, although the US economic data seems okay, and the unemployment rate has fallen from 4.5% in November to 4.4% in December last year, the number of discouraged and part-time workers is still 8.4% (compared to 8.7% in November). These figures reflect that nearly 10% of the discouraged/part-timers and 4.4% of the unemployed are experiencing anxiety and stress related to work, which of course affects their families.

We Christians are facing the same environment, but the difference lies in our perspective on these pressures and trials in Christ. The Bible says, “In all things God works for the good of those who love him” (Romans 8:28). These “all things” certainly include the good and bad we see, the favorable and unfavorable circumstances we encounter. If we believe in God’s word, we have a different attitude towards “all things.”

If we love God, we must trust that God allows “all things” to happen to us for His good purpose. If we can understand what lessons God wants us to learn, or experience His promises and help in difficult situations, we can benefit from them and regain strength to face life’s challenges.

The church is currently planning to establish a workplace referral network, aiming to connect job-seeking members with those already employed. The goal is to leverage the internal referral systems within companies where employed members work, in order to provide job opportunities for seekers.

Furthermore, if brothers and sisters wish to nominate qualified brothers and sisters for deacon candidate training, please obtain the nominee’s consent and submit the nomination, along with the signature of 2 persons, to the church office by January 31st. Please indicate “To the Ordination of Deacons Planning Committee” on the envelope.

Pastor Chiu

Prayer Requests

  • We thank God that our ministry at each church campus continues into the new year. More brothers and sisters are willing to step up in service. Let’s pray that God will help our pastors and staff to lead the congregation to fulfill the mission that God has given us.
  • We pray for brothers and sisters who are currently looking for job or considering a change of profession. Let’s ask God to guide them and enable them to follow the Holy Spirit’s guidance during their search. Through dialogue with God daily they may gradually come to understand His will and live in His love.
  • Pray for the celebrations and outreach activities that our church campuses are currently preparing for the Lunar New Year. Let’s ask that God grant our brothers and sisters the joy of working with God and others during the preparation process, and that God will bless and utilize these events.
  • Pray that God will move the brothers and sisters who are qualified to be deacon candidates, that they may have a willing heart to serve God and a humble spirit to respond to God’s call when nominated.
  • Pray for our pastors and staff. They need to take on double the work to cope with the staff shortage. Let us also pray that God will send suitable co-workers to participate in the ministry team of the church as soon as possible.

Offerings Summary

Dec. General offering $ 695,801.64
Jan. General offering $ 97,625.14
